compounds.name: Dali::Ui::AbsoluteLayout
compounds.brief: AbsoluteLayout positions its children at explicit coordinates.
compounds.detailed: Children are positioned using attached properties that specify their bounds (x, y, width, height). Supports: Absolute positioning with pixel values Proportional positioning (0.0 to 1.0 relative to parent) Proportional sizing (0.0 to 1.0 relative to parent) Common Property Behavior RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight: Only used when the corresponding bounds dimension is negative (auto-size). When bounds width or height is >= 0, the bounds value takes precedence and RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight is ignored. Alignment: Not used. Position is determined solely by the bounds x/y values and margin. Margin: Applied as an offset to the bounds position.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::AbsoluteLayoutParams
compounds.brief: AbsoluteLayoutParams stores per-child layout parameters for AbsoluteLayout.
compounds.detailed: This handle provides direct access to bounds and flags attached to a child view. Use View::SetLayoutParams() to attach parameters to a child view. view.SetLayoutParams(AbsoluteLayoutParams::New() .SetBounds(LayoutRect(10,20,100,200)) .SetFlags(AbsoluteLayoutFlags::POSITION_PROPORTIONAL));

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView
compounds.brief: AnimatedImageView is a View for displaying animated image resources such as GIF files.
compounds.detailed: It supports playback control (play, pause, stop) and loop count configuration. The animation starts automatically when Play() is called after the image is loaded. AnimatedImageViewview=AnimatedImageView::New("animation.gif"); view.SetLoopCount(3); view.Play();

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::AsyncImageLoader
compounds.brief: The AsyncImageLoader is used to load pixel data from a URL asynchronously.
compounds.detailed: The images are loaded in a worker thread to avoid blocking the main event thread. To keep track of the loading images, each load call is assigned an ID (which is returned by the Load() call). To know when the Load has completed, connect to the ImageLoadedSignal. This signal should be connected before Load is called (in case the signal is emitted immediately). Load errors can be detected by checking the PixelData object is valid from within the signal handler. Note: The PixelData object will automatically be destroyed when it leaves its scope. Example: classMyClass:publicConnectionTracker { public: MyCallback(uint32_tloadedTaskId,PixelDatapixelData) { //Firstcheckiftheimageloadedcorrectly. if(pixelData) { if(loadedTaskId==mId1) { //usetheloadedpixeldatafromthefirstimage } elseif(loadedTaskId==mId2) { //usetheloadedpixeldatafromthesecondimage } } } uint32_tmId1; uint32_tmId2; }; MyClassmyObject; AsyncImageLoaderimageLoader=AsyncImageLoader::New(); //Connectthesignalhere. imageLoader.ImageLoadedSignal().Connect(&myObject,&MyClass::MyCallback); //Invoketheloadcalls(mustdothisafterconnectingthesignaltoguaranteecallbacksoccur). myObject.mId1=imageLoader.Load("first_image_url.jpg"); myObject.mId2=imageLoader.Load("second_image_url.jpg");

members.brief: Creates an initialized BackgroundBlurEffect, using default settings. As default, blur radius is set to 10u.
members.returns: A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

members.name: New
members.brief: Creates an initialized BackgroundBlurEffect.
notes: Due to the downscale factor and kernel optimizations, the actual applied blur changes only in fixed increments.

The increment size is (2 / downscaleFactor). For example, with downscaleFactor = 0.25, the effective blur radius changes in steps of 8 pixels, so small adjustments below that threshold will have no visual effect.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Visual::Base
compounds.brief: A Visual provides a renderer for drawing a control component. A control may have multiple visuals.
compounds.detailed: Visuals reuse geometry, shader etc. across controls. They ensure that the renderer and texture sets exist only when control is on-stage. Each visual also responds to actor size and color change, and provides clipping at the renderer level. Note: The visual responds to the the Actor::COLOR by blending it with the 'Multiply' operator. The following properties are optional, but can be supplied in the property map to Dali::Ui::VisualFactory::CreateVisual(). Property Name Type customShader MAP transform MAP where customShader is a map with at least one of the following properties: | Property Name | Type | Required | Default | Description | |----------------------|-------------------------|-------|------|--------------------------------------| | vertexShader | STRING | No | "" | Vertex shader code | | fragmentShader | STRING | No | "" | Fragment shader code | | subdivideGridX | INTEGER | No | 1 | How to subdivide the grid along X | | subdivideGridY | INTEGER | No | 1 | How to subdivide the grid along Y | | shaderHints | INTEGER or ARRAY of STRING | No | NONE | Bitmask of hints and transform is a map with the following properties: | Property Name | Type | Required | Default | Description | |----------------------|----------------|-------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------| | offset | VECTOR2 | No | (0,0) | Offset of visual from origin | | size | VECTOR2 | No | (1,1) | size of visual | | origin | INTEGER or STRING | No | CENTER | origin of the visual Relative means that the component describes a factor of the parent control size; size.x = 1 means full width; size.y = 0.5 means half height. Absolute means that the component describes world units (equivalent to pixels)

If we call this API at least 1 time, we cannot set as DepthIndex::AUTO_INDEX after.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Builder
compounds.detailed: This class provides the ability to load and style an actor tree from a string representation. The following is an example in JSON. { "templates"://arenamedinstantiableactortrees { "defaultText": { "type":"TextActor", "font":"", "parentOrigin":[0.5,0.5,0], "scale":[50,50,1] } }, "styles"://arenamedpropertysetsappliedtoactortrees { "myStyle": { "size":[10,10,1]//rootpropertiesappliedtoagivenrootactor "actors"://propertiesappliedtoactorsfoundbynamefromroot { "ok"://propertiesforanactornamed"ok" { "scale":[5,5,1], }, "cancel": { "scale":[50,50,1], } }, }, }, "stage": [ { "type":"defaultText", "text":"HelloWorld", "position":[0,0,0] }, ] } The following shows a method to load the json file. Builderbuilder=Builder::New(); std::stringjson_data(ReadFile("layout.json")); builder.LoadFromString(json_data); Examples Load all actors in the "stage" section to the root layer builder.AddActors(Stage::GetCurrent().GetRootLayer()); Create an actor tree from the "templates" section TextActoractor=TextActor::DownCast(builder.Create("defaultText")); Style an actor tree from the "styles" section builder.ApplyStyle("myStyle",actor); Create an actor tree from json TextActoractor=TextActor::DownCast( builder.CreateFromJson("{\"type\":\"TextActor\",\"font\":\"\",\"scale\":[50,50,1]}")); Apply a style to an actor tree from json builder.ApplyFromJson(textActor,("{\"scale\":[5,5,1]}"));

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::DevelFocusManager::CustomAlgorithmInterface
compounds.brief: Interface used to provide custom focus algorithm for retrieving the next focusable actor.
compounds.detailed: The application / ui can implement the interface and override the focus behaviour. Upon providing an implementation of this interface, the PreFocusChangeSignal is no longer emitted. If focus is changing within a layout container, then the layout container is queried first to provide the next focusable actor. If this does not provide a valid actor, then the FocusManager will check focusable properties to determine next focusable actor. If focusable properties are not set, then the FocusManager calls the GetNextFocusableActor() method of this interface.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::DragAndDropDetector
compounds.brief: The DragAndDropDetectors provides signals when draggable objects are dragged into other object.
compounds.detailed: It provides signals for when the draggable object start drag, draggable object enters other object, moves around in other object, leaves other object, dropped into other object and finally when the drag ended. The basic usage is shown below: voidExample() { DragAndDropDetectordetector=DragAndDropDetector::New(); //Getnotificationswhenthedraggableitemstartdrag detector.StartedSignal().Connect(&OnStarted); //Getnotificationswhenthedraggableitementersotheritem detector.EnteredSignal().Connect(&OnEntered); //Getnotificationswhenthedraggableitemleavesotheritem detector.ExitedSignal().Connect(&OnExited); //Getnotificationswhenthedraggableitemismovedwithinotheritem detector.MovedSignal().Connect(&OnMoved); //Getnotificationswhenthedraggableitemisdropped detector.DroppedSignal().Connect(&OnDropped); //Getnotificationswhenthedraggableobjectdragended detector.EndedSignal().Connect(&OnEnded); } voidOnStarted(Viewview,DragAndDropDetectordetector) { //Querythenewvalues std::cout<<"Position="<<detector.GetCurrentScreenPosition()<<std::endl; } voidOnEntered(Viewview,DragAndDropDetectordetector) { //Changemodeasrequired } voidOnExited(Viewview,DragAndDropDetectordetector) { //Changemodeasrequired } voidOnMoved(Viewview,DragAndDropDetectordetector) { //Querythenewvalues std::cout<<"Position="<<detector.GetCurrentScreenPosition()<<std::endl; } voidOnDropped(Viewview,DragAndDropDetectordetector) { //Querythenewvalues std::cout<<"Position="<<detector.GetCurrentScreenPosition()<<",Content="<<detector.GetContent()<< std::endl; } voidOnEnded(Viewview,DragAndDropDetectordetector) { //Changemodeasrequired }

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Text::FitCandidate
compounds.brief: Describes a text fit candidate for candidate-based text fit.
compounds.detailed: Each fit candidate defines a font size and line height configuration that can be selected during text fit. Text fit selects the largest candidate that fits into the available layout space. The line height is specified as an absolute value. This object is used to configure candidate-based text fit behavior.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Text::FitRange
compounds.brief: Describes a font size range for range-based text fit.

Text fit searches within the configured range using the specified font size step to find the largest fitting size. The line height is not specified by this object. It follows the current text style configuration, including the configured line height mode and value. This object is used to configure text fit behavior.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::FlexLayout
compounds.brief: FlexLayout arranges its children using the CSS Flexbox algorithm.
compounds.detailed: FlexLayout provides a flexible way to lay out children in rows or columns, with support for wrapping, alignment, and flexible sizing. It supports: Direction (Row, RowReverse, Column, ColumnReverse) Wrap behavior (NoWrap, Wrap, WrapReverse) Justify content (FlexStart, FlexEnd, Center, SpaceBetween, SpaceAround, SpaceEvenly) Align items and align content

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::FocusManager
compounds.brief: Provides the functionality of handling focus navigation and maintaining the two dimensional focus chain.
compounds.detailed: It provides functionality of setting the focus and moving the focus in four directions (i.e. Left, Right, Up and Down). It also draws a highlight for the focused actor and emits a signal when the focus is changed. Signals Signal Name Method preFocusChange PreFocusChangeSignal() focusChanged FocusChangedSignal() focusGroupChanged FocusGroupChangedSignal() focusedActorEnterKey FocusedActorEnterKeySignal()

members.brief: Moves the focus to the given actor.
members.detailed: Only one actor can be focused at the same time. The actor must be in the stage already and focusable.
notes: If the parent of this actor has the KEYBOARD FOCUSABLE CHILDREN property set to false, it will not be focused.
members.returns: Whether the focus is successful or not

members.returns: true if the movement was successful

members.name: ClearFocus

members.detailed: It will emit focus changed signal without current focused actor.

members.name: PreFocusChangeSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted before the focus is going to be changed.
members.detailed: FocusManager makes the best guess for which actor to focus towards the given direction, but applications might want to change that. By connecting with this signal, they can check the proposed actor to focus and return a different actor if they wish. This signal is only emitted when the navigation key is pressed and FocusManager tries to move the focus automatically. It won't be emitted for focus movement by calling SetCurrentFocusActor directly. A callback of the following type may be connected: ActorYourCallbackName(ActorcurrentFocusedActor,ActorproposedActorToFocus,FocusDirection::Directiondirection);
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: FocusChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted after the current focused actor has been changed.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(ActororiginalFocusedActor,ActorcurrentFocusedActor);

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Text::FontVariation
compounds.brief: Utility functions for font variation settings.
compounds.detailed: This class provides helper functions to convert between string-based font variation settings and axis-based representations. A font variation settings string consists of one or more pairs of axis tags and numeric values separated by commas. Supported formats include: wght=700,wdth=90 (recommended) "wght" 700, "wdth" 90 'wght' 700, 'wdth' 90 In quoted formats, the axis tag must be wrapped with single quotes (U+0027) or double quotes (U+0022). Each axis tag must contain exactly four printable ASCII characters in the range U+0020..U+007E. If duplicate axis tags are specified, the last value is used. Parsing is strict. If the input string does not conform to the expected format, an empty axis list is returned.

members.name: FromString
members.brief: Parses a font variation settings string.
members.detailed: The input string must follow one of the supported formats described in this class documentation.
members.returns: A vector of parsed font variation axes. Returns an empty vector if parsing fails.

members.name: ToString
members.brief: Converts font variation axes to string.
members.detailed: The returned string uses the canonical format: wght=700,wdth=90
members.returns: The font variation settings string.

notes: Unsupported axis tags may be ignored depending on the selected font.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Text::FontVariationAxis
compounds.brief: Describes a single variable font axis.
compounds.detailed: A font variation axis consists of a four-character OpenType axis tag and a numeric value. Examples of axis tags include "wght", "wdth", "slnt", and "opsz". This object is used to configure font variation settings explicitly.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::GridLayout
compounds.brief: GridLayout arranges its children in a grid of rows and columns.
compounds.detailed: It supports: Row and column definitions with absolute, star (*), and auto sizing Row and column spacing Child positioning using attached properties (Row, Column, RowSpan, ColumnSpan) Common Property Behavior RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight: A positive value sets a fixed size. WRAP_CONTENT (-1) uses the child's natural size. MATCH_PARENT (-2) fills the parent. GridLayoutParams alignment: START, CENTER, and END position the child within the cell. FILL (the default) stretches the child to fill the entire cell. When a child's measured size is larger than the cell, the child is clipped to the cell bounds regardless of alignment. Margin: Applied inside the cell, reducing the space available for the child.

members.name: GetPlaceholderUrl

members.name: SetImageColor

members.detailed: Accepts both direct RGBA values and theme color tokens via UiColor.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: color
params.description: The color to multiply with the image

members.name: GetImageColor
members.brief: Gets the color multiplier applied to the image.
members.returns: The current image color

members.name: SetPixelArea
members.brief: Sets the pixel area used to display a sub-region of the image.
members.detailed: The area is specified as normalized coordinates: (x, y, width, height) where each component is in the range [0, 1].
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: pixelArea
params.description: The normalized sub-region of the image to display

members.name: GetPixelArea
members.brief: Gets the pixel area.
members.returns: The current pixel area as normalized coordinates

members.name: SetSamplingMode
members.brief: Sets the sampling mode used when scaling the image.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: GetSamplingMode
members.brief: Gets the sampling mode.
members.returns: The current sampling mode

members.name: SetFittingMode
members.brief: Sets how the image is fitted within the view bounds.
members.detailed: The default fitting mode is Ui::FittingMode::FILL (stretch to fill).
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: GetFittingMode
members.brief: Gets the fitting mode.
members.returns: The current fitting mode (default: FILL)

members.name: SetDesiredWidth
members.brief: Sets the desired rasterization width as a hint for the image loader.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: width
params.description: The desired width in pixels (0 to use natural size)

members.name: GetDesiredWidth
members.brief: Gets the desired rasterization width hint.
members.returns: The desired width in pixels, or 0 if not set

members.name: SetDesiredHeight
members.brief: Sets the desired rasterization height as a hint for the image loader.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: height
params.description: The desired height in pixels (0 to use natural size)

members.name: GetDesiredHeight
members.brief: Gets the desired rasterization height hint.
members.returns: The desired height in pixels, or 0 if not set

members.name: SetImageLoadWithViewSize
members.brief: Sets whether the image is loaded synchronously at the current view size.
members.detailed: When enabled, the image is (re)loaded at the view's resolved layout size each time the view size changes. This avoids loading the full-resolution image when only a smaller display size is needed, saving memory and decode time. Direction: view size → image load size.
notes: Do NOT use together with aspect-ratio layout on the same ImageView. ImageLoadWithViewSize causes GetNaturalSize to return the current view size instead of the true image dimensions, making aspect-ratio adjustment ineffective.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to enable loading image with view size

members.name: IsImageLoadWithViewSizeEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether the image is loaded with the view size.
members.returns: True if loading image with view size is enabled

members.name: SetPreMultipliedAlpha
members.brief: Sets whether the image uses pre-multiplied alpha.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: preMultiplied
params.description: True if the image has pre-multiplied alpha

members.name: IsPreMultipliedAlpha
members.brief: Gets whether pre-multiplied alpha is enabled.
members.returns: True if pre-multiplied alpha is enabled

members.name: SetAlphaMaskUrl
members.brief: Sets the URL of an alpha mask image.
members.detailed: When set, the alpha channel of the mask image is applied to the main image.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: maskUrl
params.description: The URL of the alpha mask image

members.name: GetAlphaMaskUrl
members.brief: Gets the alpha mask image URL.
members.returns: The URL of the alpha mask image, or an empty string if not set

members.name: SetCropToMask
members.brief: Sets whether the image should be cropped to the mask bounds.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: cropToMask
params.description: True to crop the image to the mask

members.name: IsCropToMask
members.brief: Gets whether crop-to-mask is enabled.
members.returns: True if crop-to-mask is enabled

members.name: SetMaskingMode
members.brief: Sets the masking mode.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: GetMaskingMode
members.brief: Gets the masking mode.
members.returns: The current masking mode

members.name: SetLoadPolicy
members.brief: Sets the load policy for the image resource.
members.detailed: Determines whether the image is loaded immediately when the view is created (IMMEDIATE), or deferred until the view is attached to the scene (ATTACHED).
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: GetLoadPolicy
members.brief: Gets the load policy.
members.returns: The current load policy

members.name: SetReleasePolicy
members.brief: Sets the release policy for the image resource.
members.detailed: Controls when the image texture is released from memory.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: GetReleasePolicy
members.brief: Gets the release policy.
members.returns: The current release policy

members.name: SetSynchronousLoading
members.brief: Sets whether the image is loaded synchronously.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: synchronous
params.description: True to load the image on the main thread synchronously

members.name: IsSynchronousLoading
members.brief: Gets whether synchronous loading is enabled.
members.returns: True if synchronous loading is enabled

members.name: SetFastTrackUpload
members.brief: Sets whether fast-track uploading is enabled.
members.detailed: When enabled, the image is uploaded to the GPU on a background thread to reduce main-thread stalls.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: IsFastTrackUploadEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether fast-track uploading is enabled.
members.returns: True if fast-track uploading is enabled

members.name: SetOrientationCorrection
members.brief: Sets whether EXIF orientation metadata is applied automatically.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.name: IsOrientationCorrectionEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether orientation correction is enabled.
members.returns: True if orientation correction is enabled

members.name: SetNPatchBorder
members.brief: Sets the N-patch border insets.
members.detailed: The border is specified as (left, top, right, bottom) pixel values. Setting a non-zero border activates N-patch rendering.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: border
params.description: The border insets as (left, top, right, bottom)

members.name: GetNPatchBorder
members.brief: Gets the N-patch border insets.
members.returns: The current border as (left, top, right, bottom)

members.name: SetNPatchBorderOnly
members.brief: Sets whether only the N-patch border regions are rendered.
members.detailed: When enabled, the center region of the N-patch is not rendered.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: borderOnly
params.description: True to render only the border regions

members.name: IsNPatchBorderOnly
members.brief: Gets whether border-only rendering is enabled.
members.returns: True if only the border regions are rendered

members.name: SetDepthIndex
members.brief: Sets the depth index of the image visual.
members.detailed: The depth index controls the rendering order of visuals within the same layer. Higher values are rendered on top.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: depthIndex
params.description: The depth index to assign to the image visual

members.name: GetLoadingStatus
members.brief: Gets the current loading status of the image.
members.returns: The current ResourceStatus of the image visual

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::InputField

compounds.detailed: It supports user interaction for text input and editing, and handles text layout and rendering.

members.name: SetLayoutDirectionMode
members.brief: Sets how the layout direction of the text is resolved.
members.detailed: LayoutDirectionMode::CONTENTS: The layout direction is determined from the text content itself. LayoutDirectionMode::INHERIT: The layout direction is inherited from the parent view. LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE: The layout direction is determined based on the system locale.
notes: The default layout direction mode of InputField is LayoutDirectionMode::INHERIT.

params.name: mode
params.description: The LayoutDirectionMode used to determine the text layout direction.

members.name: SetFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the font size scale.
members.detailed: The scaled font size is calculated from the current font size multiplied by this scale value.

members.name: GetFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the font size scale.
members.returns: The font size scale.

members.name: SetMinimumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the minimum font size scale.
members.detailed: If this value is greater than the maximum font size scale, the adjusted font size scale follows this minimum value.

members.name: GetMinimumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the minimum font size scale.
members.returns: The minimum font size scale.

members.name: SetMaximumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the maximum font size scale.
members.detailed: If this value is less than the minimum font size scale, the adjusted font size scale follows the minimum font size scale.

members.name: GetMaximumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the maximum font size scale.
members.returns: The maximum font size scale.

members.name: SetSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether the system font size scale is applied.
members.detailed: When enabled, the system font size scale is used instead of the user-defined font size scale before applying the minimum and maximum constraints.

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to apply the system font size scale, false otherwise.

members.name: IsSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether the system font size scale is applied.
members.returns: True if the system font size scale is applied, otherwise false.

members.name: SetFontVariation
members.brief: Sets the font variation axes.
members.detailed: This replaces all previously set font variation axes. If duplicate axis tags are provided, the last value is used. Unsupported axis tags may be ignored depending on the selected font.

members.name: SetFontVariation
members.brief: Sets the font variation from a settings string.
members.detailed: The settings string consists of one or more pairs of axis tags and numeric values separated by commas. Supported formats include: wght=700,wdth=90 (recommended) "wght" 700, "wdth" 90 'wght' 700, 'wdth' 90 In quoted formats, the axis tag must be wrapped with single quotes (U+0027) or double quotes (U+0022). Each axis tag must contain exactly four printable ASCII characters in the range U+0020..U+007E. Space is allowed only as trailing characters in the axis tag. If duplicate axis tags are specified, the last value is used. If the input string is invalid, the font variation is not changed. Unsupported axis tags may be ignored depending on the selected font.

members.name: GetFontVariation
members.brief: Returns the font variation axes.
members.returns: The font variation axes.

members.name: ClearFontVariation
members.brief: Clears the font variation.
members.detailed: This removes all previously set font variation axes.

members.name: GetAdjustedFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the adjusted font size scale used for rendering.
members.detailed: The adjusted font size scale is resolved after applying the current minimum and maximum font size scale constraints and, if enabled, the system font size scale. If the minimum font size scale is greater than the maximum font size scale, the minimum font size scale takes precedence and is used as the adjusted scale.
members.returns: The adjusted font size scale used for rendering.

members.name: TextChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the text content changes.
members.detailed: voidOnTextChanged(Viewview);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

members.name: MaximumLengthReachedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the text input reaches the maximum allowed length.
members.detailed: The signal is triggered when an attempt is made to insert additional characters beyond the configured maximum length. voidOnMaximumLengthReached(Viewview);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

members.name: CursorPositionChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the cursor position changes.
members.detailed: voidOnCursorPositionChanged(Viewview,uint32_toldPosition);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Integration::InteractiveEventReceiverInterface
compounds.brief: Interface for receiving interactive events from InteractiveTrait.
compounds.detailed: When InteractiveTrait is attached to a View whose ViewImpl implements this interface, the trait calls these methods directly before emitting the corresponding signals. This avoids the overhead of adding signal connections for subclass event handling. Typically implemented by InteractiveViewImpl and its subclasses.

members.name: OnClicked
members.brief: Called when the view is clicked.

params.name: event
params.description: The input event that triggered the click

members.name: OnPressedChanged
members.brief: Called when the pressed state changes.
members.signature: void OnPressedChanged(View view, bool pressed, const InputEvent &event)=0

params.name: view
params.description: The view whose pressed state changed

params.name: pressed
params.description: True when pressed, false when released

params.name: event
params.description: The input event that caused the state change

members.name: OnLongPressed
members.brief: Called when a long press is detected.
members.returns: True to suppress the subsequent click event, false to allow it

params.name: view
params.description: The view that received the long press

params.name: event
params.description: The input event that triggered the long press

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait
compounds.brief: InteractiveTrait is an interaction trait that provides clickable behavior to a View.
compounds.detailed: InteractiveTrait provides click, long-press, and pressed-state handling functionality. It can be attached to any View to make it interactive.

members.name: InteractiveTrait
members.brief: Creates an uninitialized Trait handle.

members.name: InteractiveTrait
members.brief: Copy constructor.
members.detailed: Creates another handle that points to the same real object.

members.name: ~InteractiveTrait
members.brief: Destructor.
members.detailed: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

members.name: PressedChangedSignal
members.brief: Emitted when the pressed state changes.
members.detailed: This signal is emitted when the view's pressed state changes due to touch or keyboard input. The pressed state is true when the user is interacting with the view (e.g., pressing down), and false when the interaction ends (e.g., releasing the touch or key).
members.signature: Signal< void(View, bool, const InputEvent &)> & PressedChangedSignal()
members.returns: The pressed changed signal

members.name: PseudoDisabledChangedSignal
members.brief: Emitted when the pseudo disabled state changes.
members.detailed: This signal is emitted when the pseudo disabled state is changed via SetPseudoDisabled(). The view is visually presented as disabled when in pseudo disabled state, but it still accepts user interaction to provide guidance or an alternative flow.
members.returns: The pseudo disabled changed signal

members.name: ClickedSignal
members.brief: Emitted when the view is clicked.
members.detailed: This signal is emitted when the view receives a tap gesture, indicating a click action. The click action is triggered when the user taps on the view.
notes: This signal may not be emitted if the one of LongPressed signal handler consumes the event (returns true).
members.returns: The clicked signal

members.name: LongPressedSignal
members.brief: Emitted when the view receives a long press gesture.
members.detailed: This signal is emitted when the user performs a long press on the view. A long press is detected when the user presses and holds on the view for a specified duration.
notes: If the handler returns true to consume the event, the Clicked signal will not be emitted.
This signal is not emitted when ClickedKeyType is set to PRESS.
members.returns: The long pressed signal

members.name: IsPressed
members.brief: The boolean flag for pressed state.
members.returns: True if the object is pressed

members.name: IsPseudoDisabled
members.brief: Checks if the view is pseudo disabled state. The view is visually presented as disabled, but it still accepts user interaction in order to provide guidance or an alternative flow.
members.returns: True if the object is pseudo disabled

members.name: SetPseudoDisabled
members.brief: Sets the pseudo disabled state to the view. The view is visually presented as disabled, but it still accepts user interaction in order to provide guidance or an alternative flow.

params.name: pseudoDisabled
params.description: True if the object is pseudo disabled

members.name: IsClickable
members.brief: Checks if clicking is currently allowed (not blocked).
members.returns: True if clicking is allowed, false if clicking is blocked

members.name: SetClickable
members.brief: Sets whether clicking is allowed.

params.name: clickable
params.description: True to allow clicking, false to block clicking

members.name: GetKeyClickPolicy
members.brief: Retrieves the current key click policy.
members.returns: The active KeyClickPolicy for this button.

members.name: SetKeyClickPolicy
members.brief: Configures the trigger timing for key-driven click events.

params.name: policy
params.description: The KeyClickPolicy to be applied (OnRelease, OnPress, or Disabled).

members.name: ConnectClickedSignal
members.brief: Connects a handler to ClickedSignal.
members.detailed: Multiple calls accumulate — each call adds an additional handler. If obj implements ConnectionTrackerInterface (e.g. inherits ConnectionTracker), the connection is automatically removed when obj is destroyed. Supports member functions and stateless lambdas: view.ConnectClickedSignal(this,&MyClass::OnButtonClicked); view.ConnectClickedSignal(this,[](Viewv,constInputEvent&e){...});
members.returns: Reference to this for method chaining

params.name: obj
params.description: The object to connect to (must implement ConnectionTrackerInterface for auto-disconnect)

params.name: func
params.description: Member function pointer or functor with signature void(View, const InputEvent&)

members.name: ConnectPressedChangedSignal
members.brief: Connects a handler to PressedChangedSignal.
members.detailed: Multiple calls accumulate — each call adds an additional handler. The handler is called with pressed=true on press-down and pressed=false on release. If obj implements ConnectionTrackerInterface, the connection is auto-removed on obj destruction.
members.returns: Reference to this for method chaining

params.name: func
params.description: Member function pointer or functor with signature void(View, bool, const InputEvent&)

members.name: ConnectLongPressedSignal
members.brief: Connects a handler to LongPressedSignal.
members.detailed: Multiple calls accumulate — each call adds an additional handler. Long press fires once when the gesture is detected (on press-down after the time threshold). Press release is reported separately via PressedChangedSignal. The handler must return bool: return true to suppress the subsequent click event, or false to allow it. If obj implements ConnectionTrackerInterface, the connection is auto-removed on obj destruction.
members.returns: Reference to this for method chaining

params.name: func
params.description: Member function pointer or functor with signature bool(View, const InputEvent&)

members.brief: Creates an initialized Trait.
members.returns: A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

members.brief: Downcasts a handle to InteractiveTrait handle.
members.detailed: If the handle refers to a InteractiveTrait (e.g. a trait from GetTrait), the downcast produces a valid handle. Otherwise the returned handle is uninitialized.
members.returns: A handle to InteractiveTrait or an uninitialized handle

params.name: handle
params.description: Handle to an object (e.g. Trait from View's interaction trait)

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::InteractiveTraitInterface
compounds.brief: Interface for interaction traits attached to a View.
compounds.detailed: An interaction trait defines how a View reacts to input focus and key events (e.g., clickable, selectable, group-selectable traits used by button-like controls). A single interaction trait is attached to a View via the reserved ReservedTraitId::INTERACTION_TRAIT identifier.

members.returns: True if the event should be consumed

members.brief: Called when the control gains or loses key input focus.

members.name: OnEnabledChanged
members.brief: Called when the control becomes enabled or disabled.
members.detailed: Allows the trait to clean up interaction state (e.g. releasing pressed state when the view becomes disabled).

params.name: enabled
params.description: True if enabled, false if disabled

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::InteractiveView
compounds.brief: InteractiveView is a View subclass with interactive behavior built in.
compounds.detailed: InteractiveView guarantees that an InteractiveTrait is attached for the lifetime of the view. It exposes the InteractiveTrait API directly, so callers do not need to go through EnsureInteractiveTrait(). InteractiveView is intended as a base class for clickable UI components such as Button and Checkbox. It can also be instantiated directly when a plain interactive container is needed.

params.name: view

params.name: pressed
params.description: True when pressed, false when released

params.name: event
params.description: The input event that caused the state change

members.name: OnLongPressed
members.brief: Called when a long press is detected on the view.
members.detailed: Override in subclasses to handle long press. Return true to suppress the subsequent click event; return false to allow it. The default implementation returns false.
members.signature: bool OnLongPressed(Ui::View view, const Ui::InputEvent &event) override
members.returns: True to suppress the subsequent click, false to allow it

params.name: view
params.description: The view that received the long press

params.name: event
params.description: The input event that triggered the long press

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Label
compounds.brief: Label is a non-editable View that displays text.
compounds.detailed: It performs text layout and rendering using the text rendering backend, but does not support user interaction or text editing.

members.name: Label
members.brief: Creates an uninitialized Label handle.
members.detailed: Only derived versions can be instantiated. Calling member functions with an uninitialized Dali::Object is not allowed.

members.name: Label
members.brief: Copy constructor.
members.detailed: Creates another handle that points to the same real object.

members.name: Label
members.brief: Move constructor.

members.name: ~Label
members.brief: Virtual destructor.
members.detailed: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

members.brief: Copy assignment operator.
members.detailed: Changes this handle to point to another real object.
members.returns: Reference to this

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Move assignment operator.
members.returns: Reference to this

params.description: The text to display in UTF-8 format.

members.returns: The font family currently set on the label.

members.name: GetFontSize

members.returns: The font size currently set on the label, in pixels.

members.name: SetMultiLine
members.brief: Sets whether the text should be multi-line.

params.name: multiLine
params.description: True for multi-line layout, false for single-line layout.

members.name: IsMultiLine
members.brief: Gets whether the text should be multi-line.
members.returns: True if multi-line layout is enabled, otherwise false.

members.name: SetLineWrapMode
members.brief: Sets the line wrap mode.

params.name: mode
params.description: The line wrap mode to apply.

members.name: GetLineWrapMode
members.brief: Gets the line wrap mode.
members.returns: The current line wrap mode.

members.name: SetTextColor
members.brief: Sets the color of the text.

members.name: GetTextColor
members.brief: Gets the color of the text.
members.returns: The text color currently set on the label.

members.name: SetHorizontalTextAlignment
members.brief: Sets the horizontal alignment of the text within the label.

members.name: GetHorizontalTextAlignment
members.brief: Gets the horizontal text alignment.
members.returns: The horizontal text alignment.

members.name: SetVerticalTextAlignment
members.brief: Sets the vertical alignment of the text within the label.

members.name: GetVerticalTextAlignment
members.brief: Gets the vertical text alignment.
members.returns: The vertical text alignment.

members.name: SetOverflowMode
members.brief: Sets the overflow mode.

members.name: GetOverflowMode
members.brief: Gets the overflow mode.
members.returns: The current overflow mode.

members.name: SetLineHeight
members.brief: Sets the line height of the text.
members.detailed: The interpretation of this value depends on the current LineHeightMode. If the mode is LineHeightMode::RELATIVE, the line height is calculated as a multiplier of the font pixel size: CalculatedLineHeight(px)=FontSize(px)*lineHeight If the mode is LineHeightMode::ABSOLUTE, the value is treated as an absolute line height in pixels. Setting lineHeight to LINE_HEIGHT_AUTO uses the natural line height derived from the font metrics, regardless of the current LineHeightMode. This behavior is similar to the "Auto" line height option in design tools such as Figma.
notes: The final line height is clamped to be no smaller than the natural line height derived from the font metrics.

members.name: GetLineHeight
members.brief: Gets the current line height value.
members.detailed: The returned value is interpreted according to the current LineHeightMode. A value of -1.0f indicates that the natural line height (based on font metrics) is used.
members.returns: The line height value.

members.name: SetLineHeightMode
members.brief: Sets how the line height value is interpreted.
members.detailed: LineHeightMode::RELATIVE: The line height is calculated as a multiplier of the font size. LineHeightMode::ABSOLUTE: The line height is treated as an absolute pixel value. The default mode is LineHeightMode::RELATIVE.

members.name: GetLineHeightMode
members.brief: Gets the current line height mode.
members.returns: The current LineHeightMode.

members.name: SetLayoutDirectionMode
members.brief: Sets how the layout direction of the text is resolved.
members.detailed: LayoutDirectionMode::CONTENTS: The layout direction is determined from the text content itself. LayoutDirectionMode::INHERIT: The layout direction is inherited from the parent view. LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE: The layout direction is determined based on the system locale.

params.name: mode
params.description: The LayoutDirectionMode used to determine the text layout direction.

members.name: GetLayoutDirectionMode
members.brief: Gets the current layout direction mode.
members.returns: The LayoutDirectionMode used to resolve the text layout direction.

members.name: SetMarkupEnabled
members.brief: Enables or disables mark-up processing for the text.

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to enable mark-up parsing, false to render text as plain string.

members.name: IsMarkupEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether mark-up processing is enabled.
members.returns: True if mark-up parsing is enabled, otherwise false.

members.name: SetAnchorColor
members.brief: Sets the color of anchors in the text.

params.name: color
params.description: The color to apply to anchors.

members.name: GetAnchorColor
members.brief: Gets the color of anchors in the text.
members.returns: The current anchor color.

members.name: SetAnchorClickedColor
members.brief: Sets the color of anchors when they are clicked.

params.name: color
params.description: The color to apply to clicked anchors.

members.name: GetAnchorClickedColor
members.brief: Gets the color of anchors when they are clicked.
members.returns: The current clicked anchor color.

members.name: SetMarqueeTriggerPolicy
members.brief: Sets how the marquee animation is triggered.
members.detailed: MarqueeTriggerPolicy::MANUAL: The marquee starts only when StartMarquee() is explicitly called. MarqueeTriggerPolicy::ON_OVERFLOW: The marquee starts automatically during layout when the text exceeds the available space. The default policy is MarqueeTriggerPolicy::MANUAL.

members.name: GetMarqueeTriggerPolicy
members.brief: Returns when the marquee animation is triggered.
members.returns: The current marquee trigger policy.

members.name: SetMarqueeSpeed
members.brief: Sets the marquee speed.

params.name: speed
params.description: The marquee speed in pixels per second.

members.name: GetMarqueeSpeed
members.brief: Returns the marquee speed.
members.returns: The marquee speed in pixels per second.

members.name: SetMarqueeLoopCount
members.brief: Sets the number of complete loops for marquee.

members.name: GetMarqueeLoopCount
members.brief: Returns the number of complete loops for marquee.
members.returns: The number of loops.

members.name: SetMarqueeLoopDelay
members.brief: Sets the amount of time to delay the start of marquee and further loops.

members.name: GetMarqueeLoopDelay
members.brief: Returns the amount of time to delay the start of marquee and further loops.
members.returns: The delay time in seconds.

members.name: SetMarqueeGap
members.brief: Sets the gap before marquee wraps.

members.name: GetMarqueeGap
members.brief: Returns the gap before marquee wraps.
members.returns: The gap in pixels.

members.name: SetMarqueeOrientation
members.brief: Sets the marquee orientation.
members.detailed: Horizontal is applied only for single-line text, and vertical only for multi-line text. The setting is ignored if the text layout does not match the required condition.

members.name: GetMarqueeOrientation
members.brief: Returns the marquee orientation.
members.returns: The marquee orientation.

members.name: SetMarqueeStopMode
members.brief: Sets how the marquee stops.

members.name: GetMarqueeStopMode
members.brief: Returns how the marquee stops.
members.returns: The marquee stop mode.

members.name: SetFontWeight
members.brief: Sets the font weight.

members.name: GetFontWeight
members.brief: Returns the font weight.
members.returns: The font weight.

members.name: SetFontWidth
members.brief: Sets the font width.

members.name: GetFontWidth
members.brief: Returns the font width.
members.returns: The font width.

members.name: SetFontSlant
members.brief: Sets the font slant.

members.name: GetFontSlant
members.brief: Returns the font slant.
members.returns: The font slant.

members.name: SetTextBackgroundColor
members.brief: Sets the background color behind the text.
members.detailed: The background is rendered behind the glyphs of the text.

members.name: GetTextBackgroundColor
members.brief: Gets the background color behind the text.
members.returns: The current text background color.

members.name: ClearTextBackgroundColor
members.brief: Clears the text background color.
members.detailed: Disables the text background and removes the previously set color.

members.name: SetUnderline
members.brief: Sets the underline style.

members.name: ClearUnderline
members.brief: Clears the underline style.

members.name: SetShadow
members.brief: Sets the shadow style.

members.name: ClearShadow
members.brief: Clears the shadow style.

members.name: SetOutline
members.brief: Sets the outline style.

members.name: ClearOutline
members.brief: Clears the outline style.

members.name: SetLineThrough
members.brief: Sets the line-through style.

members.name: ClearLineThrough
members.brief: Clears the line-through style.

members.name: SetBevel
members.brief: Sets the bevel style.

members.name: ClearBevel
members.brief: Clears the bevel style.

members.name: SetTextFit
members.brief: Sets the text fit range.
members.detailed: Text fit selects the largest font size within the configured range that fits into the available layout space.
notes: Text fit is designed for bounded layout sizes. It is recommended to explicitly specify width and height when using this feature. When width or height is WRAP_CONTENT, measurement is performed using the maximum font size in the configured range.

members.name: SetTextFit
members.brief: Sets the text fit candidates.
members.detailed: Text fit selects the largest candidate that fits into the available layout space. Each candidate defines a font size and line height.
notes: Text fit is designed for bounded layout sizes. It is recommended to explicitly specify width and height when using this feature. When width or height is WRAP_CONTENT, measurement uses the maximum fit candidate to determine the size. The maximum candidate is selected by the largest font size, and if equal, by the larger line height.

params.name: candidates
params.description: The vector of text fit candidates.

members.name: ClearTextFit
members.brief: Clears the text fit configuration.

members.name: SetFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the font size scale.
members.detailed: The scaled font size is calculated from the current font size multiplied by this scale value.

members.name: GetFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the font size scale.
members.returns: The font size scale.

members.name: SetMinimumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the minimum font size scale.
members.detailed: If this value is greater than the maximum font size scale, the adjusted font size scale follows this minimum value.

members.name: GetMinimumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the minimum font size scale.
members.returns: The minimum font size scale.

members.name: SetMaximumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the maximum font size scale.
members.detailed: If this value is less than the minimum font size scale, the adjusted font size scale follows the minimum font size scale.

members.name: GetMaximumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the maximum font size scale.
members.returns: The maximum font size scale.

members.name: SetSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether the system font size scale is applied.
members.detailed: When enabled, the system font size scale is combined with the current font size scale before applying the minimum and maximum constraints.

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to apply the system font size scale, false otherwise.

members.name: IsSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether the system font size scale is applied.
members.returns: True if the system font size scale is applied, otherwise false.

members.name: SetFontVariation
members.brief: Sets the font variation axes.
members.detailed: This replaces all previously set font variation axes. If duplicate axis tags are provided, the last value is used. Unsupported axis tags may be ignored depending on the selected font.

members.name: SetFontVariation
members.brief: Sets the font variation from a settings string.
members.detailed: The settings string consists of one or more pairs of axis tags and numeric values separated by commas. Supported formats include: wght=700,wdth=90 (recommended) "wght" 700, "wdth" 90 'wght' 700, 'wdth' 90 In quoted formats, the axis tag must be wrapped with single quotes (U+0027) or double quotes (U+0022). Each axis tag must contain exactly four printable ASCII characters in the range U+0020..U+007E. Space is allowed only as trailing characters in the axis tag. If duplicate axis tags are specified, the last value is used. If the input string is invalid, the font variation is not changed. Unsupported axis tags may be ignored depending on the selected font.

members.name: GetFontVariation
members.brief: Returns the font variation axes.
members.returns: The font variation axes.

members.name: ClearFontVariation
members.brief: Clears the font variation.
members.detailed: This removes all previously set font variation axes.

members.name: SetCutoutEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether the text is rendered as a cutout.
members.detailed: When enabled, the glyph shapes are cut out from the rendered content instead of being filled with the text color.

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to render the text as a cutout, false to render it normally.

members.name: IsCutoutEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether the text is rendered as a cutout.
members.returns: True if the text is rendered as a cutout, otherwise false.

members.name: SetMaskEffect
members.brief: Applies a mask effect using the given view.
members.detailed: This helper creates a MaskEffect using the rendered output of the view as the mask source and applies it to the label. The given view is added as a child of the label and retained internally until ClearMaskEffect() is called.
notes: Any existing RenderEffect on the label will be replaced.

params.name: view
params.description: The view used as the mask source.

members.name: ClearMaskEffect
members.brief: Clears the mask effect applied to the label.
members.detailed: Removes the internally retained mask view from the label and clears the applied RenderEffect.

members.name: SetAsyncRendering
members.brief: Sets whether the text is rendered asynchronously.
members.detailed: When enabled, the label automatically requests asynchronous text rendering during relayout as needed. By default, text is rendered synchronously.
notes: The render result is delivered through Label::AsyncRenderFinishedSignal().

params.name: asyncRendering
params.description: True to enable asynchronous text rendering, false to render text synchronously.

members.name: IsAsyncRendering
members.brief: Gets whether asynchronous text rendering is enabled.
members.returns: True if asynchronous text rendering is enabled, otherwise false.

members.name: SetRenderScale
members.brief: Sets the render scale of the text.
members.detailed: Renders text by rasterizing glyphs at a larger scale and downscaling the result. This improves rendering quality when the view is visually scaled, by reducing quality loss caused by texture upscaling. The layout size of the view is not affected. Valid only when async rendering is enabled, and the value must be 1.0f or greater.

members.name: GetRenderScale
members.brief: Gets the render scale of the text.
members.returns: The current render scale.

members.name: GetLineCount
members.brief: Gets the number of lines of text within the current layout width.
notes: The line count is calculated based on the current width of the label, clamped between its minimum and maximum width. If the width is not yet resolved (e.g., when using wrap content or match parent constraints), it may be zero before layout is completed, which can result in an incorrect line count.
members.returns: The number of lines.

members.name: GetLineCount
members.brief: Gets the number of lines of text within the given width.
members.returns: The number of lines.

params.name: width
params.description: The width used to calculate the line count.

members.name: GetAsyncLineCount
members.brief: Gets the line count from the most recent asynchronous result.
notes: This value is updated when an asynchronous render or asynchronous size computation completes.
members.returns: The number of lines from the most recent asynchronous result.

members.name: IsMarqueeRunning
members.brief: Returns whether the marquee animation is currently running.
members.returns: True if the marquee animation is running, false otherwise.

members.name: GetAdjustedFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the adjusted font size scale used for rendering.
members.detailed: The adjusted font size scale is resolved after applying the current minimum and maximum font size scale constraints and, if enabled, the system font size scale. If the minimum font size scale is greater than the maximum font size scale, the minimum font size scale takes precedence and is used as the adjusted scale.
members.returns: The adjusted font size scale used for rendering.

members.name: StartMarquee
members.brief: Starts the marquee animation using the current marquee settings.
members.detailed: The marquee starts only when the orientation matches the current text layout: horizontal for single-line text, and vertical for multi-line text. If the condition is not met, this call has no effect.
notes: If the trigger policy is MarqueeTriggerPolicy::ON_OVERFLOW, the marquee starts only when the text exceeds the available space.

members.name: StopMarquee
members.brief: Stops the marquee animation.
members.detailed: The stopping behavior follows the current MarqueeStopMode: MarqueeStopMode::IMMEDIATE: Stops the marquee immediately. MarqueeStopMode::FINISH_LOOP: Continues the animation until the current loop finishes, then stops. This method works regardless of the current MarqueeTriggerPolicy. It stops the marquee even if the policy is MANUAL or ON_OVERFLOW.

members.name: RequestAsyncNaturalSize
members.brief: Requests asynchronous natural size computation.
members.detailed: This method can be used regardless of whether asynchronous rendering is enabled. The computed natural size includes the label padding.
notes: The computed result is delivered through Label::AsyncNaturalSizeComputedSignal().

members.name: RequestAsyncHeightForWidth
members.brief: Requests asynchronous height-for-width computation.
members.detailed: This method can be used regardless of whether asynchronous rendering is enabled. The given width must be the total label width including padding.
notes: The computed result is delivered through Label::AsyncHeightForWidthComputedSignal().

params.name: width
params.description: The total width used for the computation, including padding.

members.name: AnchorClickedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when an anchor in the text is clicked.
members.detailed: voidOnAnchorClicked(Viewview,constDali::String&href);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

members.name: AsyncRenderFinishedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when asynchronous text rendering is finished.
members.detailed: voidOnAsyncRenderFinished(Viewview,floatwidth,floatheight);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

members.name: AsyncNaturalSizeComputedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when asynchronous natural size computation is finished.
members.detailed: voidOnAsyncNaturalSizeComputed(Viewview,floatwidth,floatheight);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

members.name: AsyncHeightForWidthComputedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when asynchronous height-for-width computation is finished.
members.detailed: voidOnAsyncHeightForWidthComputed(Viewview,floatwidth,floatheight);
members.returns: The signal to connect to.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::LabelAnimationBridge
compounds.brief: A lightweight bridge for applying animations to a Label.
compounds.detailed: LabelAnimationBridge extends ViewAnimationBridge with Label-specific animation properties. All parent properties are also available with proper return type for fluent chaining. Typically created via Label::Animate(): autoanim=Animation::New(); label.Animate(anim) .Opacity(0.5f,300_ms); anim.Play();

members.name: GetHorizontalTextAlignment

members.name: SetVerticalTextAlignment
members.brief: Sets the vertical alignment of the text within the label.

members.name: GetVerticalTextAlignment
members.brief: Gets the vertical text alignment.
members.returns: The vertical text alignment.

members.name: SetOverflowMode
members.brief: Sets the overflow mode.

members.name: GetOverflowMode
members.brief: Gets the overflow mode.
members.returns: The current overflow mode.

members.name: SetLineHeight
members.brief: Sets the line height of the text.
members.detailed: The interpretation of this value depends on the current LineHeightMode. If the mode is LineHeightMode::RELATIVE, the line height is calculated as a multiplier of the font pixel size: CalculatedLineHeight(px)=FontSize(px)*lineHeight If the mode is LineHeightMode::ABSOLUTE, the value is treated as an absolute line height in pixels. Setting lineHeight to LINE_HEIGHT_AUTO uses the natural line height derived from the font metrics, regardless of the current LineHeightMode. This behavior is similar to the "Auto" line height option in design tools such as Figma.
notes: The final line height is clamped to be no smaller than the natural line height derived from the font metrics.

members.name: GetLineHeight
members.brief: Gets the current line height value.
members.detailed: The returned value is interpreted according to the current LineHeightMode. A value of -1.0f indicates that the natural line height (based on font metrics) is used.
members.returns: The line height value.

members.name: SetLineHeightMode
members.brief: Sets how the line height value is interpreted.
members.detailed: - LineHeightMode::RELATIVE: The line height is calculated as a multiplier of the font size. LineHeightMode::ABSOLUTE: The line height is treated as an absolute pixel value. The default mode is LineHeightMode::RELATIVE.

members.name: GetLineHeightMode
members.brief: Gets the current line height mode.
members.returns: The current LineHeightMode.

members.name: SetLayoutDirectionMode
members.brief: Sets how the layout direction of the text is resolved.
members.detailed: - LayoutDirectionMode::CONTENTS: The layout direction is determined from the text content itself. LayoutDirectionMode::INHERIT: The layout direction is inherited from the parent view. LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE: The layout direction is determined based on the system locale.

params.name: mode
params.description: The LayoutDirectionMode used to determine the text layout direction.

members.name: GetLayoutDirectionMode
members.brief: Gets the current layout direction mode.
members.returns: The LayoutDirectionMode used to resolve the text layout direction.

members.name: SetMarkupEnabled

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to enable mark-up parsing, false to render text as plain string.

members.name: IsMarkupEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether mark-up processing is enabled.
members.returns: True if mark-up parsing is enabled, otherwise false.

members.brief: Sets how the marquee animation is triggered.
members.detailed: - MarqueeTriggerPolicy::MANUAL: The marquee starts only when StartMarquee() is explicitly called. MarqueeTriggerPolicy::ON_OVERFLOW: The marquee starts automatically during layout when the text exceeds the available space. The default policy is MarqueeTriggerPolicy::MANUAL.

members.returns: The current marquee trigger policy.

members.name: GetMarqueeLoopDelay

members.detailed: Horizontal is applied only for single-line text, and vertical only for multi-line text. The setting is ignored if the text layout does not match the required condition.

members.brief: Sets the font size scale.
members.detailed: The scaled font size is calculated from the current font size multiplied by this scale value.

members.name: GetFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the font size scale.
members.returns: The font size scale.

members.name: SetMinimumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the minimum font size scale.
members.detailed: If this value is greater than the maximum font size scale, the adjusted font size scale follows this minimum value.

members.name: GetMinimumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the minimum font size scale.
members.returns: The minimum font size scale.

members.name: SetMaximumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Sets the maximum font size scale.
members.detailed: If this value is less than the minimum font size scale, the adjusted font size scale follows the minimum font size scale.

members.name: GetMaximumFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the maximum font size scale.
members.returns: The maximum font size scale.

members.name: SetSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether the system font size scale is applied.
members.detailed: When enabled, the system font size scale is combined with the current font size scale before applying the minimum and maximum constraints.

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to apply the system font size scale, false otherwise.

members.name: IsSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether the system font size scale is applied.
members.returns: True if the system font size scale is applied, otherwise false.

members.name: GetFontVariation
members.brief: Returns the font variation axes.
members.returns: The font variation axes.

members.name: ClearFontVariation
members.brief: Clears the font variation.
members.detailed: This removes all previously set font variation axes.

members.name: RegisterFontVariationProperty
members.brief: Registers or retrieves a font variation property for the given axis tag.
members.detailed: If a property with the same tag is already registered, the existing property is reused and its current value is updated.
members.returns: The property index associated with the variation tag, or Property::INVALID_INDEX on failure.

members.name: SetCutoutEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether the text is rendered as a cutout.
members.detailed: When enabled, the glyph shapes are cut out from the rendered content instead of being filled with the text color.

params.name: enabled
params.description: True to render the text as a cutout, false to render it normally.

members.name: IsCutoutEnabled
members.brief: Gets whether the text is rendered as a cutout.
members.returns: True if the text is rendered as a cutout, otherwise false.

members.name: SetLetterSpacing
members.brief: Sets the additional spacing between letters in pixels.
members.detailed: Positive values increase the spacing, while negative values reduce it.

params.name: spacing
params.description: The additional letter spacing in pixels.

members.name: GetLetterSpacing
members.brief: Gets the additional spacing between letters in pixels.
members.returns: The additional letter spacing in pixels.

members.name: SetMaskEffect
members.brief: Applies a mask effect using the given view.
members.detailed: This helper creates a MaskEffect using the rendered output of the view as the mask source and applies it to the label. The given view is added as a child of the label and retained internally until ClearMaskEffect() is called.
notes: Any existing RenderEffect on the label will be replaced.

params.name: view
params.description: The view used as the mask source.

members.name: ClearMaskEffect
members.brief: Clears the mask effect applied to the label.
members.detailed: Removes the internally retained mask view from the label and clears the applied RenderEffect.

members.name: SetAsyncRendering
members.brief: Sets whether the text is rendered asynchronously.
members.detailed: When enabled, the label automatically requests asynchronous text rendering during relayout as needed. By default, text is rendered synchronously.
notes: The render result is delivered through Label::AsyncRenderFinishedSignal().

params.name: asyncRendering
params.description: True to enable asynchronous text rendering, false to render text synchronously.

members.name: IsAsyncRendering
members.brief: Gets whether asynchronous text rendering is enabled.
members.returns: True if asynchronous text rendering is enabled, otherwise false.

members.name: SetRenderScale
members.brief: Sets the render scale of the text.
members.detailed: Renders text by rasterizing glyphs at a larger scale and downscaling the result. This improves rendering quality when the view is visually scaled, by reducing quality loss caused by texture upscaling. The layout size of the view is not affected. Valid only when async rendering is enabled, and the value must be 1.0f or greater.

members.name: GetRenderScale
members.brief: Gets the render scale of the text.
members.returns: The current render scale.

members.name: IsMarqueeRunning
members.brief: Returns whether the marquee animation is currently running.
members.returns: True if the marquee animation is running, false otherwise.

members.name: GetAdjustedFontSizeScale
members.brief: Gets the adjusted font size scale used for rendering.
members.detailed: The adjusted font size scale is resolved after applying the current minimum and maximum font size scale constraints and, if enabled, the system font size scale. If the minimum font size scale is greater than the maximum font size scale, the minimum font size scale takes precedence and is used as the adjusted scale.
members.returns: The adjusted font size scale used for rendering.

members.name: StartMarquee
members.brief: Starts the marquee animation using the current marquee settings.
members.detailed: The marquee starts only when the orientation matches the current text layout: horizontal for single-line text, and vertical for multi-line text. If the condition is not met, this call has no effect.
notes: If the trigger policy is MarqueeTriggerPolicy::ON_OVERFLOW, the marquee starts only when the text exceeds the available space.

members.name: StopMarquee
members.brief: Stops the marquee animation.
members.detailed: The stopping behavior follows the current MarqueeStopMode: MarqueeStopMode::IMMEDIATE: Stops the marquee immediately. MarqueeStopMode::FINISH_LOOP: Continues the animation until the current loop finishes, then stops. This method works regardless of the current MarqueeTriggerPolicy. It stops the marquee even if the policy is MANUAL or ON_OVERFLOW.

members.name: RequestAsyncNaturalSize
members.brief: Requests asynchronous natural size computation.
members.detailed: This method can be used regardless of whether asynchronous rendering is enabled. The computed natural size includes the label padding.
notes: The computed result is delivered through Label::AsyncNaturalSizeComputedSignal().

members.name: RequestAsyncHeightForWidth
members.brief: Requests asynchronous height-for-width computation.
members.detailed: This method can be used regardless of whether asynchronous rendering is enabled. The given width must be the total label width including padding.
notes: The computed result is delivered through Label::AsyncHeightForWidthComputedSignal().

params.name: width
params.description: The total width used for the computation, including padding.

members.name: RequestAsyncRenderWithFixedSize
members.brief: Requests asynchronous text rendering with a fixed size.

members.name: RequestAsyncRenderWithFixedWidth
members.brief: Requests asynchronous text rendering with a fixed width.

members.name: RequestAsyncRenderWithFixedHeight
members.brief: Requests asynchronous text rendering with a fixed height.

members.name: RequestAsyncRenderWithConstraints
members.brief: Requests asynchronous text rendering within the given constraints.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Layout
compounds.brief: Layout is a View that arranges child views using a layout algorithm.
compounds.detailed: Layout extends View and owns a LayoutManager that defines the layout algorithm. Only Layout (and its derived classes like StackLayout, GridLayout) can have a layout algorithm. To customize measure/arrange behavior, use SetMeasureCallback() and SetArrangeCallback() on View. Child management uses Actor::Add/Remove (inherited from View's base class). Insert(index, View) and RemoveAllChildren() are provided by View.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::LayoutController
compounds.brief: Layout controller manages layout invalidation and processing.
compounds.detailed: The layout controller is responsible for scheduling layout passes when layouts become invalid and processing them during the render loop. It integrates with the DALi adaptor system as a processor. Each Window has its own LayoutController instance. The controller batches layout requests and processes them once per frame to optimize performance.

members.name: Get
members.brief: Gets the layout controller instance for a window.
members.detailed: Creates the controller if it doesn't exist for the given window.
members.returns: Reference to the layout controller

params.name: window
params.description: The window to get the controller for

members.name: Remove
members.brief: Removes the layout controller for the given window.
members.detailed: Should be called when a window is being closed to release the associated LayoutController and prevent stale entries.

params.name: window
params.description: The window whose controller should be removed

members.brief: Unregisters a view from all known layout controllers.
members.detailed: Used when a view is being destroyed but its window cannot be determined (e.g. the view is already off-scene).

members.name: ~LayoutController
members.brief: Destructor.

members.name: RequestLayout
members.brief: Schedules a view with layout capability for layout processing.
members.detailed: Layout roots are views that have a LayoutManager and are at the top of the layout hierarchy (e.g. directly under the window). The controller will batch these requests and process them during the next frame.

params.name: view
params.description: The view with layout capability to schedule

members.name: UnregisterView
members.brief: Unregisters a view from the layout controller.
members.detailed: Should be called when a layout root is being destroyed to prevent dangling pointer access.

members.name: OnWindowResize
members.brief: Called when the window is resized.
members.detailed: This invalidates all layout roots and triggers a layout pass.

members.name: ProcessLayouts
members.brief: Processes all pending layout requests immediately.
members.detailed: This is called automatically by the system once per frame, but can be called manually if immediate layout is needed.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Integration::LayoutControllerImpl
compounds.brief: Internal implementation of LayoutController.
compounds.detailed: Implements the Integration::Processor interface to hook into DALi's per-frame processing pipeline.

members.name: LayoutControllerImpl
members.brief: Constructor.

members.name: ~LayoutControllerImpl
members.brief: Destructor.

members.name: RequestLayout
members.brief: Schedules a view for layout processing.
members.detailed: Any View that is a layout root (has LayoutManager or children) can be registered.

members.name: UnregisterView
members.brief: Removes a view from tracking (called when view is destroyed).

members.name: GetCurrentWindow
members.brief: Gets the current window handle managed by this layout controller.
members.detailed: Retrieves the window that this layout controller instance is associated with. This is used internally to verify if the window has been replaced.
members.returns: The current window handle

members.name: ReplaceCurrentWindow
members.brief: Replaces the current window with a new one.
members.detailed: Updates the layout controller to manage a different window instance. This is called when a window object has been replaced but the same LayoutController instance should continue managing layouts for the new window. The method reconnects the window resize signal to ensure layout invalidation continues to work correctly.

members.name: OnWindowResize
members.brief: Called when window is resized.

members.name: ProcessLayouts
members.brief: Processes all pending views with layout capability.

members.name: Process
members.brief: Implementation of Integration::Processor::Process.
members.detailed: Called once per frame by DALi's adaptor.

members.name: GetProcessorName
members.brief: Implementation of Integration::Processor::GetProcessorName.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Integration::LayoutImpl

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::LayoutManager
compounds.brief: Abstract base class for layout managers.
compounds.detailed: Layout managers are responsible for measuring and arranging child views within a container. They implement the specific layout algorithms for different layout types (stack, grid, flex, etc.). LayoutManager works with ViewImpl for direct access to implementation details during layout calculations. This allows any View (or derived class) to have layout capability.

members.name: ~LayoutManager
members.brief: Virtual destructor.

members.name: LayoutManager
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.brief: Measures the view and its children.
members.detailed: This method calculates the desired size of the view based on the constraints and the sizes of its children.
members.signature: MeasuredSize Measure(ViewImpl *view, float widthConstraint, float heightConstraint)=0
members.returns: The measured size

members.name: ArrangeChildren
members.brief: Arranges the children within the view bounds.
members.detailed: This method positions and sizes the children within the given bounds according to the layout algorithm.
members.returns: The final arranged size

params.name: view
params.description: The internal view whose children to arrange

params.name: bounds
params.description: The available bounds for arranging children

members.name: MeasureChild
members.brief: Measures a child view.
members.detailed: Helper method for derived classes to measure a child view.

members.returns: The measured size of the child

params.name: widthConstraint
params.description: The width constraint for the child

params.name: heightConstraint
params.description: The height constraint for the child

members.name: ArrangeChild
members.brief: Arranges a child view within the given bounds.
members.detailed: Helper method for derived classes to arrange a child view.
members.returns: The final arranged size

members.name: GetChildren
members.brief: Gets the children container from a view.
members.detailed: Helper method for derived classes to access children.
members.returns: Reference to the children container

params.name: view
params.description: The view to get children from

notes: This is an internal class. It is not intended to be used directly by applications.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::LottieAnimationView

compounds.detailed: It renders JSON-based Lottie animation files and supports playback control (play, pause, stop), loop configuration, and frame-range control. LottieAnimationViewview=LottieAnimationView::New("animation.json"); view.SetLoopCount(-1);//infinite view.Play();

members.detailed: Ownership of callback is transferred to the visual after SetDynamicProperty() is called.
notes: The callback is invoked on a worker thread. Do NOT call DALi APIs from it.

members.brief: Animation finished signal type. Emitted when the animation completes all loops.

members.detailed: The animation will play between minFrame and maxFrame.

members.name: SetMinMaxFrameByMarker
members.brief: Sets the playback range using marker names embedded in the Lottie file.
members.detailed: If only minMarker is given, the animation plays the range of that marker. If both are given, the animation plays from the start of minMarker to the end of maxMarker.
members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

members.brief: Sets the speed factor for animation playback.

members.returns: Reference to this for fluent chaining

params.name: redraw
