members.brief: Returns whether redraw on scale-down is enabled.

members.name: SetRedrawOnScaleUp

members.brief: Returns whether redraw on scale-up is enabled.

members.brief: Enables or disables frame caching.
members.detailed: When enabled, all decoded frames are cached in memory to reduce CPU cost during looping, at the expense of higher memory usage.

params.name: enable

members.name: SetRenderScale
members.brief: Sets the scale factor applied to the rasterization size.
members.detailed: A value of 2.0 rasterizes at twice the visual dimensions, producing sharper output on high-density displays at the cost of higher memory usage. Negative values flip the image. The actual clamping is handled by the underlying animation renderer.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::MaskEffect
compounds.brief: MaskEffect is a visual effect that masks owner View. This class is a concrete class from RenderEffect interface. Add this effect to a view, clear manually to deactivate.
compounds.detailed: Ui::View view = Ui::View::New(); parent.Add(view); view.SetRenderEffect(MaskEffect::New(maskView)); // Activate ... view.ClearRenderEffect(); // Deactivate

members.name: MaskMode
members.brief: Enumeration for selecting how the mask source interprets pixel data.

enumvalues.name: ALPHA
enumvalues.brief: Uses the alpha channel of the mask texture. (Default)

enumvalues.name: LUMINANCE
enumvalues.brief: Converts RGB to grayscale and uses the luminance as mask value.

members.name: New
members.brief: Creates an initialized MaskEffect with view, using default settings. The default settings are:
members.detailed: maskMode = MaskMode::ALPHA maskPosition = Vector2(0.f, 0.f) maskScale = Vector2(1.f, 1.f)
members.returns: A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

params.name: maskView
params.description: The source View to affect mask.

members.name: New

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::ScrollView
compounds.brief: ScrollView is a scrollable container that displays content larger than its viewport.
compounds.detailed: ScrollView provides pan gesture support for navigating content that exceeds the view's visible area, based on OneUI Scrollable component design. Key features: Pan gesture detection for scrolling Configurable scroll direction (vertical/horizontal/both) Content management Scroll position control with animation Fling/inertia scrolling with velocity tracking Edge effects for over-scrolling Scroll event signals Drag event signals Basic usage: //CreateaScrollView ScrollViewscrollView=ScrollView::New() .SetSize(400.0f,300.0f); //Createcontentviewlargerthanthescrollview Viewcontent=View::New() .SetSize(800.0f,600.0f) .BackgroundColor(Color::WHITE); scrollView.SetContent(content); //Connecttoscrollsignals scrollView.ScrollStartedSignal().Connect(this,&MyClass::OnScrollStarted); scrollView.ScrollingSignal().Connect(this,&MyClass::OnScrolling); scrollView.ScrollFinishedSignal().Connect(this,&MyClass::OnScrollFinished);

members.brief: Scroll started signal type.

members.brief: Scroll finished signal type.

members.name: ScrollingSignalType
members.brief: Scrolling signal type.

members.name: DragStartedSignalType
members.brief: Drag started signal type.

members.name: DragFinishedSignalType
members.brief: Drag finished signal type.

members.name: DraggingSignalType
members.brief: Dragging signal type.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Integration::ScrollViewLayoutManager
compounds.brief: Implements the scroll view layout algorithm.
compounds.detailed: ScrollViewLayoutManager allows content to have its natural size without being constrained by the parent size, except when the child uses MatchParent in which case the ScrollView's size is used as constraint. This layout manager is designed for ScrollView to enable scrolling of content that can be larger than the viewport.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::SelectableTrait
compounds.brief: SelectableTrait is a state trait that provides selection behavior to a View.
compounds.detailed: SelectableTrait manages a boolean selected state and emits a signal when the state changes. It can optionally toggle the selected state on click by leveraging the owner View's InteractiveTrait. Unlike InteractiveTrait, SelectableTrait does not handle key events or focus directly. It uses a separate reserved trait slot (SELECTABLE_TRAIT) and can coexist with InteractiveTrait on the same View.

members.returns: The selection changed signal

params.description: True to enable toggle-by-click, false to disable

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::StackLayout
compounds.brief: StackLayout arranges its children in a single row or column.
compounds.detailed: StackLayout is a simple layout container that stacks its children either horizontally or vertically with optional spacing between them. Common Property Behavior For a vertical StackLayout (horizontal is analogous with axes swapped): RequestedHeight (main-axis): A positive value sets a fixed height. WRAP_CONTENT (-1) uses the child's natural size. MATCH_PARENT (-2) fills the parent. RequestedWidth (cross-axis): A positive value sets a fixed width. WRAP_CONTENT (-1) uses the child's natural size. MATCH_PARENT (-2) fills the cross-axis. StackLayoutParams alignment (cross-axis): START, CENTER, and END position the child within the cross-axis. Margin: Applied on all sides, reducing the space available for the child. Weight (via StackLayoutParams): Children with weight > 0 share the remaining main-axis space proportionally. The child's main-axis RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight is ignored — the size is determined entirely by the weight ratio. Only children with weight 0 are measured normally using their RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::StateEvent
compounds.brief: Describes a single View state transition, passed to StateChangedSignal handlers.
compounds.detailed: Provides the previous and current ViewState together with ergonomic helpers for detecting specific transitions, and optional information about the input event that caused the state change. view.StateChangedSignal.Connect(this,[](View,constStateEvent&e){ if(e.Added(ViewState::FOCUSED)){...}//gainedfocus if(e.Removed(ViewState::PRESSED)){...}//released });

members.name: StateEvent
members.brief: Creates an uninitialized StateEvent handle.

members.name: ~StateEvent
members.brief: Destructor.

members.name: GetPrev
members.brief: Returns the state before this transition.

members.name: GetCurrent
members.brief: Returns the state after this transition (the current state).

members.name: Added
members.brief: Returns true if state was newly added in this transition.
members.detailed: Equivalent to: !GetPrev().Contains(state) && GetCurrent().Contains(state)

members.name: Removed
members.brief: Returns true if state was removed in this transition.
members.detailed: Equivalent to: GetPrev().Contains(state) && !GetCurrent().Contains(state)

members.name: Changed
members.brief: Returns true if state was either added or removed in this transition.

members.name: GetCause
members.brief: Returns the input event that caused this transition.
members.detailed: Always returns a valid InputEvent. Programmatic or system-triggered state changes return an InputEvent with InputEventType::NONE.

members.name: GetInputEventType
members.brief: Returns the type of the input event that caused this transition.
members.detailed: Convenience shortcut for GetCause().GetInputEventType(). Returns InputEventType::NONE if the change was programmatic or system-triggered.

members.name: GetTouchEvent
members.brief: Returns the touch event that caused this transition.

members.name: GetKeyEvent
members.brief: Returns the key event that caused this transition.

members.name: GetTapGesture
members.brief: Returns the tap gesture that caused this transition.

members.name: GetLongPressGesture
members.brief: Returns the long-press gesture that caused this transition.

members.name: GetWheelEvent
members.brief: Returns the wheel event that caused this transition.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::UiColor
compounds.brief: A color value that can hold either direct RGBA components or a string identifier that is resolved at runtime via the current theme.
compounds.detailed: When constructed with RGBA values, UiColor behaves like a lightweight wrapper around Vector4. When constructed with a string color ID, the actual RGBA values are looked up from the current ThemeLoaderInterface at the point of Resolve(). //DirectRGBA UiColorred(1.0f,0.0f,0.0f,1.0f); //ID-based(resolvedfromcurrenttheme) UiColorprimary("Primary"); //PredefinedcolorIDs view.SetBackgroundColor(UiColor::PRIMARY);

members.name: UiColor
members.brief: Creates a default UiColor (transparent black, no ID).

members.name: UiColor
members.brief: Creates a UiColor with explicit RGBA components.
members.signature: UiColor(float r, float g, float b, float a=1.0f)

members.name: UiColor
members.brief: Creates a UiColor from a Vector4.

members.name: UiColor
members.brief: Creates a UiColor with a string identifier.
members.detailed: The actual RGBA value is resolved from the current theme when Resolve() is called.

members.name: UiColor
members.brief: Creates a UiColor with a string identifier (rvalue overload).
members.detailed: The actual RGBA value is resolved from the current theme when Resolve() is called. This overload enables moving the string into the internal token registry to avoid an extra copy when the caller provides an rvalue.

members.name: UiColor
members.brief: Creates a UiColor from a hex RGB value.

members.name: HasColorId
members.brief: Returns whether this UiColor holds a string color ID.
members.returns: True if this UiColor was constructed with a color ID

members.name: GetColorId
members.brief: Returns the color ID string.
members.returns: The color ID, or an empty string if this is a direct RGBA color

members.name: Resolve
members.brief: Resolves and returns the RGBA value as a Vector4.
members.detailed: If this UiColor has a color ID, the value is looked up from the current theme. If the ID is not found, returns the fallback mColor value (default transparent black). If this UiColor has direct RGBA values, returns them as-is.
members.returns: The resolved RGBA color

members.name: operator Vector4
members.brief: Implicit conversion to Vector4.
members.detailed: Equivalent to calling Resolve().

members.name: ScaleAlpha
members.brief: Returns a UiColor with alpha scaled by the given factor.
members.detailed: RGBA: out.a = in.a * factor Token: the resolved alpha is scaled by factor

members.name: WithAlpha
members.brief: Returns a UiColor with alpha replaced by the given value.
members.detailed: RGBA: out.a = alpha Token: the resolved alpha is replaced with alpha

compounds.brief: Provides public access to the global color table.
compounds.detailed: UiColorManager is a Handle-based singleton that exposes color table operations (query, update) to application developers. The underlying color table and View binding management is handled internally. //Queryacolor Vector4bg=UiColorManager::Get().GetColor("bg_primary");

members.brief: Creates an uninitialized UiColorManager handle.

members.brief: Destructor.

members.brief: Looks up a single color by its identifier.

members.brief: Looks up a color by its identifier.

members.name: RegisterBinding
members.brief: Registers a named color binding for an object.
members.detailed: Associates callback with the given bindingId. The callback is invoked whenever the theme changes and a color has been set via UpdateBinding(). If a binding with the same bindingId already exists, it is replaced. Does not invoke the callback immediately. Ownership of callback is transferred to the manager. manager.RegisterBinding(Self(),"BackgroundColor",ColorCallback::New(this,&MyImpl::SetBg));
members.signature: void RegisterBinding(BaseHandle view, StringView bindingId, ColorCallback callback)

params.name: bindingId
params.description: A caller-defined identifier for this binding (e.g. "BackgroundColor")

params.name: callback
params.description: A ColorCallback created via ColorCallback::New(), invoked on color table change to apply the resolved color

members.name: GetBindingColor
members.brief: Retrieves the UiColor associated with a named binding.
members.signature: bool GetBindingColor(BaseHandle view, StringView bindingId, UiColor &outColor) const
members.returns: true if a binding was found, false otherwise

params.name: bindingId
params.description: The binding identifier used in UpdateBinding

members.name: HasBinding
members.brief: Returns whether a named binding exists for an object.
members.signature: bool HasBinding(BaseHandle view, StringView bindingId) const
members.returns: true if a binding with the given id is registered

members.name: SetBindingColor
members.brief: Sets the color for an existing named binding.
members.detailed: Stores color so the callback is invoked with the resolved value on theme change. If no binding with bindingId exists, this call is a no-op.
members.signature: void SetBindingColor(BaseHandle view, StringView bindingId, const UiColor &color)

params.name: bindingId
params.description: The binding identifier used in RegisterBinding

params.name: color
params.description: The new UiColor to store (must have a color ID)

members.name: ClearBinding
members.brief: Removes a named binding.

params.name: bindingId
params.description: The binding identifier used in UpdateBinding

members.name: ClearBindings
members.brief: Removes all bindings associated with a given object.

params.name: view
params.description: The target object to unbind completely

members.name: SetColorOverride
members.brief: Sets a function that overrides theme color lookups.
members.detailed: When set, this function is invoked before querying the theme loader for every color lookup. If the function returns true, the output color is used and the theme loader is skipped. If it returns false, the normal theme lookup proceeds. Setting the override immediately refreshes all existing View bindings so that the overridden colors are applied without delay. boolMyOverride(StringViewid,Vector4&out){ if(id=="Primary"){out=Vector4(1,0,0,1);returntrue;} returnfalse; } UiColorManager::Get().SetColorOverride(MyOverride);
notes: This parameter is a plain function pointer (ColorOverrideFunc). Lambdas with captures and member function pointers are not accepted. Only free functions or stateless lambdas may be used.
Passing nullptr clears the override, equivalent to calling ClearColorOverride(). All View bindings are refreshed to use theme values.
The function may be called on any thread that invokes GetColor() or triggers a theme change. The implementation must be thread-safe if colors are queried from multiple threads.

params.name: func
params.description: A function pointer matching the ColorOverrideFunc signature, or nullptr to clear the override

members.name: ClearColorOverride
members.brief: Clears the color override callback.
members.detailed: All View bindings are immediately refreshed to use the theme values.

members.name: Get
members.brief: Returns the singleton UiColorManager instance.
members.detailed: Creates the instance on first call.
members.returns: A handle to the UiColorManager singleton

members.name: DownCast
members.brief: Downcasts a handle to a UiColorManager handle.
members.returns: A handle to a UiColorManager or an uninitialized handle

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::UiConfig
compounds.brief: An immutable-after-init configuration object for dali-ui global settings.
compounds.detailed: UiConfig holds display and scaling parameters that affect unit calculations (spx, dp, sdp) across the entire dali-ui framework. Setter methods may only be called before the config is applied via Apply(). After that point, any setter call will trigger an assertion failure. Setters return a reference to this object to support fluent method chaining: intmain(intargc,char**argv) { Applicationapplication=Application::New(&argc,&argv); UiConfig::New() .SetScalingFactor(1.5f) .SetDpi(320) .Apply(); HelloWorldControllertest(application); application.MainLoop(); return0; }

members.name: BrokenImageType
members.brief: The type of broken image for image loading failures.
members.detailed: Different broken image types allow different broken images to be used based on the size of the view that needs to display them. For example, a small icon view can use a compact broken image (SMALL), while a large image view can use a more detailed broken image (LARGE).

enumvalues.name: SMALL
enumvalues.brief: Broken image for small-sized views.

enumvalues.name: NORMAL
enumvalues.brief: Broken image for normal-sized views.

enumvalues.name: LARGE
enumvalues.brief: Broken image for large-sized views.

members.name: Apply
members.brief: Applies the UiConfig as the global configuration for given application.
members.detailed: This must be called exactly once, typically in main() before the application main loop starts. After this call, the config is frozen and its setter methods can no longer be used. Unit literals (_spx, _dp, _sdp) require this to be called before they can be used. Calling Apply() more than once triggers an assertion failure.

members.name: SetExecutionKeyPredicate
members.brief: Sets the predicate used to determine whether a key event triggers click execution (e.g. "Return", "KP_Enter").
members.detailed: When a key event is received by a clickable View, this predicate is called with the key name. If it returns true, the View treats the key as a click execution key and fires the click action. The default predicate matches "Return" only. boolMyKeyPredicate(constDali::String&keyName){ returnkeyName=="Return"||keyName=="KP_Enter"; } UiConfig::New() .SetExecutionKeyPredicate(MyKeyPredicate) .Apply();
notes: This parameter is a plain function pointer (ExecutionKeyPredicate). Lambdas with captures and member function pointers are not accepted. Only free functions or stateless lambdas may be used.
Passing nullptr restores the default predicate that matches "Return".
members.returns: Reference to this UiConfig for method chaining

params.name: predicate
params.description: A function pointer matching the ExecutionKeyPredicate signature, or nullptr to restore the default

members.name: GetExecutionKeyPredicate
members.brief: Retrieves the execution key predicate.
members.returns: The current execution key predicate function pointer

members.name: SetKeyLongPressThreshold
members.brief: Sets the threshold for recognizing a key long-press.
members.detailed: When a key is held down, the system generates repeated key press events. This threshold defines how many consecutive key press events must be received before the input is recognized as a long-press action.

params.name: threshold
params.description: The minimum number of consecutive key press events required to trigger a long-press

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::UiThemeManager
compounds.brief: Singleton manager that provides access to the current theme.
compounds.detailed: UiThemeManager is a Handle-based singleton. Theme-level queries (theme ID, theme change notification) are exposed here. Color lookups are accessed through UiColorManager, not directly through this class. The ThemeLoaderInterface instance is created via UiConfigImpl::CreateThemeLoader() during UiConfig initialization. If CreateThemeLoader() returns nullptr, a built-in default loader is used.

members.name: SetLayoutMode
members.brief: Sets the layout mode of this View.
members.detailed: LayoutMode::DEFAULT (default) lets the parent container's layout system manage this View's size and position normally. LayoutMode::STANDALONE excludes this View from the parent's accumulation, spacing and index calculations. The View's size is still measured normally (so MATCH_PARENT, WRAP_CONTENT and explicit RequestedWidth/Height all work), but its position is taken from SetPositionX/SetPositionY instead of being decided by the parent layout. This is useful for floating overlays, drag previews, tooltips and absolute positioning inside any LayoutManager. Standalone children ignore the parent's padding entirely. Their measured size is the parent's full inner size minus the child's own margin, and their final position is SetPositionX/SetPositionY plus the child's own margin in the parent's coordinate space. This keeps size and position consistent: a Standalone child with MATCH_PARENT fills the parent edge to edge regardless of parent padding, and any margin set on the child shifts it inward in both axes.

members.brief: Lowers this view to the bottom of its sibling list.

params.name: policy
params.description: UPDATE also reorders the parent layout's children; PRESERVE keeps layout order unchanged and affects only visual z-order.

members.name: RaiseAbove
members.brief: Raises this view above the given target sibling.

params.name: target
params.description: The target sibling view to raise above.

params.name: policy
params.description: UPDATE also reorders the parent layout's children; PRESERVE keeps layout order unchanged and affects only visual z-order.

members.name: LowerBelow
members.brief: Lowers this view below the given target sibling.

params.name: target
params.description: The target sibling view to lower below.

params.name: policy
params.description: UPDATE also reorders the parent layout's children; PRESERVE keeps layout order unchanged and affects only visual z-order.

members.name: GetLayoutParams
members.brief: Retrieves the layout parameters of a specific type attached to this View.
members.detailed: Returns the stored handle. Modifying the returned handle directly changes the internal data. Call InvalidateMeasure() afterwards if the layout needs to be recalculated. autoparams=view.GetLayoutParams<AbsoluteLayoutParams>(); if(params) { params.SetWidth(200.0f); view.InvalidateMeasure(); }
members.returns: A valid handle if the params are attached, or an uninitialized handle

members.name: GetState
members.brief: Gets the current state of this View.
members.returns: The current ViewState (may contain multiple combined states)

members.name: StateChangedSignal
members.brief: Returns the state changed signal.
members.detailed: Emitted whenever the view's ViewState changes. view.StateChangedSignal().Connect(tracker,[](Viewv,constStateEvent&e){ if(e.Added(ViewState::FOCUSED)){...} });
members.returns: The StateChangedSignal

members.name: EnsureInteractiveTrait
members.brief: Ensures this View has an interaction trait and returns it.
members.detailed: If no interaction trait is set, an InteractiveTrait is attached and returned. If an InteractiveTrait is already attached, it is returned. If a different interaction trait is set, an assertion may fire and an empty handle is returned.
members.returns: InteractiveTrait handle, or an uninitialized handle on error

members.name: IsInteractive
members.brief: Returns whether this View has an interaction trait attached.
members.returns: True if this View has an InteractiveTrait

members.name: EnsureSelectableTrait
members.brief: Ensures this View has a selectable trait and returns it.
members.detailed: If no selectable trait is set, a SelectableTrait is attached and returned. If a SelectableTrait is already attached, it is returned. If a different selectable trait type is set, an assertion may fire and an empty handle is returned.
members.returns: SelectableTrait handle, or an uninitialized handle on error

members.name: IsSelectable
members.brief: Returns whether this View has a selectable trait attached.
members.returns: True if this View has a SelectableTrait

members.name: SetInteractionEffect
members.brief: Sets an interaction effect on this view.
members.detailed: The effect is applied when the view becomes interactive (via AsInteractive()). If the view is already interactive, the effect is applied immediately. The effect is frozen at set time — subsequent modifications to the original handle do not affect this view. Pass Trait{} to remove the current effect. If this method is called before AsInteractive(), the explicitly set effect takes priority over the UiConfig default.
members.returns: Reference to this View for method chaining

params.name: effect
params.description: A Trait implementing InteractionEffectInterface, or Trait{} to remove

members.name: ClearBackground
members.brief: Clears the background.

members.name: SetRenderEffect
members.brief: Sets RenderEffect to this View.
notes: Every effect inherits RenderEffect.

members.name: GetRenderEffect
members.brief: Retrieves View's render effect.
members.returns: RenderEffect set on this View

members.name: ClearRenderEffect
members.brief: Clears RenderEffect of this View, if exists.

members.name: IsResourceReady
members.brief: Query if all resources required by a View are loaded and ready.
members.detailed: Most resources are only loaded when the View is placed on stage.
members.returns: true if the resources are loaded and ready, false otherwise

members.name: IsOnScene
members.brief: Checks whether the view is connected to the scene.
members.returns: true if the view is on scene, false otherwise

members.name: ResourceReadySignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted after all resources required by a View are loaded and ready.
members.detailed: Most resources are only loaded when the View is placed on stage. If resources are shared between ImageViews, they are cached. In this case, the ResourceReady signal may be sent before there is an object to connect to. To protect against this, IsResourceReady() can be checked first. autonewView=View::New(); newView.SetResource(resourceUrl); if(newView.IsResourceReady()) { //dosomething } else { newView.ResourceReadySignal.Connect(....) } A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Viewview);
notes: A RelayoutRequest is queued by View before this signal is emitted
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: OffScreenRenderingFinishedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when offscreen rendering is finished.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Viewview);
notes: This signal is emitted when the offscreen rendering task is completed.
This signal is only emitted when OffScreenRenderingType is set to RENDER_ONCE.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: KeyEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when key event is received.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(Viewview,constKeyEvent&event); The return value of True, indicates that the event should be consumed. Otherwise the signal will be emitted on the next parent of the actor.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: FocusChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the view gets or loses focus.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(Viewview,boolfocused);
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: Animate
members.brief: Creates a ViewAnimationBridge for this View.
members.detailed: Returns a lightweight, stack-allocated proxy that applies animations directly to the given Animation for this View. autoanim=Animation::New(); view.Animate(anim).Opacity(0.5f,300_ms); anim.Play();
members.returns: A ViewAnimationBridge

members.name: VerifyCustomActorPointer
members.brief: Template to allow deriving Views to verify whether the Internal::CustomActor* is actually an implementation of their class.

members.name: New
members.brief: Creates an initialized View.
members.returns: A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

members.name: DownCast
members.brief: Downcasts a handle to View handle.
members.detailed: If handle points to a View, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized.
members.returns: A handle to a View or an uninitialized handle

members.name: NewAnimationSpec
members.brief: Creates a new ViewAnimationSpec.
members.detailed: autospec=View::NewAnimationSpec() .Opacity(1.0f,300_ms); spec.ApplyTo(anim,view);
members.returns: A new ViewAnimationSpec

members.name: DownCast
members.brief: Template to allow deriving Views to DownCast handles to deriving handle classes.
members.returns: Handle to a class T or an uninitialized handle

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::ViewImpl
compounds.brief: This is the internal implementation class for View.
compounds.detailed: ViewImpl extends CustomActorImpl to provide basic UI functionality. It implements the p-impl pattern and provides the actual implementation for the View public API. View can optionally have a LayoutManager to manage child layout. When a LayoutManager is set, the View can manage children and delegate layout calculations to the LayoutManager.

members.returns: True if the view itself or at least one ancestor is focused

members.name: SetLayoutMode
members.brief: Sets the layout mode of this View.
members.detailed: LayoutMode::DEFAULT (default) lets the parent container's layout system manage this View's size and position normally. LayoutMode::STANDALONE excludes this View from the parent's accumulation, spacing and index calculations. The View's size is still measured normally (so MATCH_PARENT, WRAP_CONTENT and explicit RequestedWidth/Height all work), but its position is taken from SetPositionX/SetPositionY instead of being decided by the parent layout. This is useful for floating overlays, drag previews, tooltips and absolute positioning inside any LayoutManager. Standalone children ignore the parent's padding entirely. Their measured size is the parent's full inner size minus the child's own margin, and their final position is SetPositionX/SetPositionY plus the child's own margin in the parent's coordinate space. This keeps size and position consistent: a Standalone child with MATCH_PARENT fills the parent edge to edge regardless of parent padding, and any margin set on the child shifts it inward in both axes.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::ViewState
compounds.brief: ViewState is a value type representing one or more view states as a bitmask.
compounds.detailed: Predefined states (Focused, Pressed, Disabled, etc.) are registered at static initialization time. Custom states can be created at runtime via Create(). States can be combined with operator+, removed with operator-, and tested with Contains() or HasIntersectionWith(). //Singlestatecheck if(state.Contains(ViewState::PRESSED)){...} //Compositestate autoselectedPressed=ViewState::SELECTED+ViewState::PRESSED; //Customstate staticconstViewStateLoading=ViewState::Create("Loading");

members.name: ALL
members.brief: Represents all possible states.

members.name: NORMAL
members.brief: No state (0)

members.name: FOCUSED
members.brief: View has keyboard focus.

members.name: PRESSED
members.brief: View is being pressed (touch or key)

members.name: DISABLED
members.brief: View is disabled.

members.name: PSEUDO_DISABLED
members.brief: View appears disabled but still interactive.

members.name: SELECTED
members.brief: View is selected.

members.name: SELECTED_PRESSED
members.brief: SELECTED + PRESSED.

members.name: DISABLED_SELECTED
members.brief: DISABLED + SELECTED.

members.name: SELECTED_FOCUSED
members.brief: SELECTED + FOCUSED.

members.name: ViewState
members.brief: Creates a Normal state (no bits set).

members.name: Contains
members.brief: Checks if this state fully contains the specified state.
members.returns: True if all bits of other are set in this state

members.name: HasIntersectionWith
members.brief: Checks if there is any overlap between this and the other state.
members.returns: True if any common bit is set

members.name: IsNormal
members.brief: Checks if this is the Normal state (no bits set).
members.returns: True if no state bits are set

members.name: IsAnyDisabled
members.brief: Checks if this state contains DISABLED or PSEUDO_DISABLED.
members.returns: True if either DISABLED or PSEUDO_DISABLED is set

members.name: ToString
members.brief: Returns a human-readable string representation for debugging.
members.returns: A string like "Focused, Pressed" or "Normal"

members.name: WasAdded
members.brief: Checks if this state was newly added (not in prev, present in cur).
members.signature: bool WasAdded(const ViewState &prev, const ViewState &cur) const

members.name: WasRemoved
members.brief: Checks if this state was removed (present in prev, not in cur).
members.signature: bool WasRemoved(const ViewState &prev, const ViewState &cur) const

members.name: WasChanged
members.brief: Checks if this state was either added or removed.
members.signature: bool WasChanged(const ViewState &prev, const ViewState &cur) const

members.name: AnyChanged
members.brief: Checks if any bit in this state changed between prev and cur.
members.detailed: Unlike WasChanged(), this works with composite states — it returns true if any constituent bit was toggled.
members.signature: bool AnyChanged(const ViewState &prev, const ViewState &cur) const

members.name: Create
members.brief: Registers and creates a custom state with the given name.
members.detailed: If the name was already registered, the same bitmask is returned. "Normal" always returns zero, "All" always returns full mask.
members.returns: The ViewState with the assigned bitmask

params.name: name
params.description: The unique name for the state

members.name: AddPrecompileShader
members.brief: Adds a list of pre-compiled shaders to the visual factory.
members.detailed: This function allows you to add the desired precompile shader to the list. you can set it through PropertyMap. you need to know the values for setting well to use them, so please refer to the explanation below. shaderType: Set the desired shader type. dali provides these type: "image","text","color","3d" and "custom" shaderOption: Set the desired shader option. dali provides these flag: dali provides a lot of shader options, so user need to check proper shader option. vertexShader: Set the vertext shader that user want. this is for custom shader. fragmentShader: Set the fragment shader that user want. this is for custom shader. shaderName: if user want to set shader name, use this. this is for custom shader.(optional) (example) // Precompile image and Custom shader // Image Case Property::Map imageShader; imageShader["shaderType"] = "image"; imageShader["shaderOption"] = Property::Map().Add("YUV_AND_RGB", true); imageShader["shaderName"] = "IMAGE_SHADER_ROUNDED_CORNER_YUV_TO_RGB"; // Text Case Property::Map textShader; textShader["shaderType"] = "text"; textShader["shaderOption"] = Property::Map() .Add("MULTI_COLOR", true) .Add("OVERLAY", true); // Color Case Property::Map colorShader; colorShader["shaderType"] = "color"; colorShader["shaderOption"] = Property::Map() .Add("CUTOUT", true) .Add("BORDERLINE", true); // Custom Case Property::Map customShader; customShader["shaderType"] = "custom"; customShader["vertexShader"] = "..."; // input vertexShader code customShader["fragementShader"] = "..."; // input fragementShader code customShader["shaderName"] = "MyCustomShader"; // Add to Precompile Shader VisualFactory factory = VisualFactory::Get(); factory.AddPrecompileShader(imageShader); factory.AddPrecompileShader(textShader); factory.AddPrecompileShader(colorShader); factory.AddPrecompileShader(customShader); factory.UsePreCompiledShader(); If you want to detail information about shader option. please refer to precompil-shader-option.h.
members.returns: True if shader is successfully added to the precompiled list, false otherwise.

members.name: UsePreCompiledShader
members.brief: Compile the visual shader in advance. Afterwards, when a visual using a new shader is requested, the pre-compiled shader is used.
notes: It is recommended that this method be called at the top of the application code.

Using precompiled shaders is helpful when the application is complex and uses many different styles of visual options. On the other hand,if most visuals are the same and the application is simple, it may use memory unnecessarily or slow down the application launching speed.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Integration::WebViewImpl
compounds.brief: This is the internal implementation class for WebView.
compounds.detailed: WebViewImpl wraps the dali-adaptor WebEngine and adapts its interface to the dali-ui conventions: Dali::String instead of std::string, and Dali::Ui::Callback<> instead of std::function.

members.name: InputEventType

enumvalues.brief: The input event is from touch event.

enumvalues.brief: The input event is from key event.

enumvalues.brief: The input event is from tap gesture.

enumvalues.name: WHEEL_EVENT

members.name: KeyClickPolicy
members.brief: Defines the policy for when a click event is triggered via key input. This policy is specifically for D-Pad, Remote, or Keyboard interactions.

enumvalues.name: ON_RELEASE
enumvalues.brief: Trigger click when the key is released.

enumvalues.name: ON_PRESS
enumvalues.brief: Trigger click immediately when the key is pressed.

enumvalues.name: DISABLED
enumvalues.brief: Disables click events from key inputs.

enumvalues.name: RESERVED
enumvalues.brief: Reserved for future expansion.

members.name: LayoutAlignment
members.brief: Enumeration for layout alignment options.

enumvalues.name: FILL
enumvalues.brief: Fill the available space.

enumvalues.name: START
enumvalues.brief: Align to the start (left/top)

enumvalues.name: CENTER
enumvalues.brief: Align to the center.

enumvalues.name: END
enumvalues.brief: Align to the end (right/bottom)

members.name: LayoutMode
members.brief: Layout mode of a View.
members.detailed: Default: the parent container's layout system manages this View's size and position (measure/arrange follow the parent's layout rules and the View participates in the parent's accumulation, spacing and index calculations). Standalone: the parent container excludes this View from its accumulation/spacing/ index calculations. The View's size is still measured normally (so MATCH_PARENT, WRAP_CONTENT and explicit RequestedWidth/Height work as usual), but its position is taken from the View's own SetPositionX/SetPositionY (RequestedPositionX/Y) instead of being decided by the parent's layout. Useful for floating overlays, drag previews, tooltips and absolute positioning within an arbitrary LayoutManager.

enumvalues.name: DEFAULT
enumvalues.brief: Managed by the parent container's layout (default).

enumvalues.name: STANDALONE
enumvalues.brief: Excluded from parent's layout accumulation; uses SetPositionX/SetPositionY.

members.name: StackOrientation

enumvalues.name: VERTICAL
enumvalues.brief: Vertical stacking (top to bottom)

enumvalues.name: HORIZONTAL
enumvalues.brief: Horizontal stacking (left to right)

enumvalues.name: ROW

enumvalues.name: ROW_REVERSE
enumvalues.brief: Right to left.

enumvalues.name: COLUMN
enumvalues.brief: Top to bottom.

enumvalues.name: COLUMN_REVERSE
enumvalues.brief: Bottom to top.

enumvalues.name: PRESERVE
enumvalues.brief: Keep layout children order unchanged; only visual z-order is affected.

members.name: ColorOverrideFunc
members.brief: Function pointer type for overriding theme color lookups.
members.detailed: When registered via SetColorOverride(), this function is called before querying the theme loader for every color lookup. If the function returns true, the output color is used and the theme loader is skipped. If it returns false, the normal theme lookup proceeds.

members.name: ColorCallback
members.brief: Typed callback for applying a resolved color to a View.
members.detailed: Pass to UpdateBinding() to register a binding between a UiColor and a View. manager.UpdateBinding(color,view,ColorCallback::New(this,&MyImpl::SetColor));

members.name: ExecutionKeyPredicate
members.brief: Function pointer type for determining whether a key triggers click execution.

members.name: WRAP_CONTENT
members.brief: Constant representing wrap-content sizing.
members.detailed: When RequestedWidth or RequestedHeight is set to this value (the default), the View sizes to fit its content (natural size or children bounding box).

members.name: MATCH_PARENT
members.brief: Constant representing match-parent sizing.
members.detailed: When RequestedWidth or RequestedHeight is set to this value, the View fills the parent container's available space on that axis.

members.name: CreateAlphaDiscardEffect
members.brief: Creates a new Alpha discard effect.
members.detailed: Alpha discard effect is used to discard fragments when the alpha colour value is below a threshold. This is useful for stenciling. Usage example: ImageView actor = ImageView::New( EXAMPLE_IMAGE_PATH ); Property::Map alphaDiscardEffect = CreateAlphaDiscardEffect(); actor.SetProperty( ImageView::Property::IMAGE, alphaDiscardEffect );
members.returns: A property map of the required shaders.

members.name: DissolveEffectSetCentralLine
members.brief: Set the dissolve central line.
members.detailed: Use one point (position) and one direction ( displacement ) vector to define this line As we use the texture coordinate as pixel position to calculate random offset, the line should passing through rectangle {(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)}, so make the position parameter with two component values between 0.0 to 1.0
members.signature: void DissolveEffectSetCentralLine(Actor &actor, const Vector2 &position, const Vector2 &displacement, float initialProgress)

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor that registers the uniform properties

params.name: position
params.description: The point ( locates within rectangle {(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)} ) passed through by the central line

params.name: displacement
params.description: The direction of the central line

params.name: initialProgress
params.description: The normalised initial progress of the shader

members.name: CreateDissolveEffect
members.brief: Create a new Dissolve effect.
members.detailed: DissolveEffect is a custom shader effect to achieve Dissolve effects in image views. Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uPercentage" - This value is proportional to the distortion applied; a value of zero means no distortion.
members.returns: The newly created Property::Map with the dissolve effect

params.name: useHighPrecision
params.description: True if using high precision in fragment shader for fully random noise, false otherwise

members.name: CreateDistanceFieldEffect
members.detailed: Creates a new DistanceFieldEffect DistanceFieldEffect is a custom shader effect to achieve distance field on Image actors Animatable/Constrainable uniforms - These will need to be registered to the actor as a custom property to take into effect: "uDoGlow" - The glow state. If true, glow is enabled "uGlowBoundary" - The glow boundary factor "uGlowColor" - The glow color multiplier "uDoShadow" - The shadow state. If true, shadows is enabled. Cannot be used with glow/and or outline "uShadowColor" - The shadow color multiplier "uShadowOffset" - The shadow offset "uDoOutline" - The outline state. If true, outline is enabled "uOutlineColor" - The outline color multiplier "uOutlineParams"- Thickness of outline. The outline thickness is determined by two values. First value [0-1] Specifies the distance field value for the center of the outline. Second value [0-1] Specifies the softness/width/anti-aliasing of the outlines inner edge.
members.returns: The newly created Property::Map with the distance field effect

members.name: CreateImageRegionEffect
members.brief: Creates a new ImageRegionEffect.
members.detailed: ImageRegionEffect is a custom shader effect to show only a region of an Image actor. Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uTopLeft" - The top-left corner of the image region. The coordinates are in percentage, (0,0) being the top-left and (1,1) the bottom right of the original image "uBottomRight" - The bottom-right corner of the image region. The coordinates are in percentage, (0,0) being the top-left and (1,1) the bottom right of the original image
members.returns: A property map of the required shader

members.name: SetMotionBlurProperties
members.brief: Set the properties for the motion blur.

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor that registers the uniform properties

params.name: numBlurSamples
params.description: Number of samples used by the shader

members.name: CreateMotionBlurEffect
members.brief: Create a new MotionBlurEffect.
members.detailed: Motion blur shader works on a per object basis. Objects will blur when they move, or if the camera moves. Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uBlurTexCoordScale" - This scales the offset for texture samples along the motion velocity vector. A smaller value means the samples will be spaced closer, larger value further apart. User should use this to get the blur to look contiguous, i.e. the blur texels should not be too widely spread, with gaps in between. Default 0.125. "uGeometryStretchFactor" - This scales the amount the geometry stretches backwards along the motion velocity vector. A smaller value means the geometry stretches less, larger it stretches more. User should use this to get the blur to 'bleed' into areas outside the physical bounds of the actor. We need this as the blur is only applied inside the bounds of the actor, but you would expect motion blur trails where the actor was previously but is there no longer. Default 0.05. "uSpeedScalingFactor" - This takes the magnitude of the motion velocity vector and scales it to produce a value which is used to fade the blur in / out with the speed that the actor is moving. As the blur fades in, more of the blur is visible and less of the original actor, and viceversa. This value is also used to control how much to fade the actor near the edges, based on the speed the actor is moving. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.5. "uObjectFadeStart" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will start to fade towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the blurred actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.25, which is half way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uObjectFadeEnd" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will finish fading towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the blurred actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied.Default 0.5, which is all the way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uAlphaScale" - Global scaler applied to the alpha of the actor. Used to make the blurred actor a bit more subtle (helps to hide discontinuities due to limited number of texture samples) and reveal a bit of the background behind it as it moves. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.75. "uNumSamples" - The number of texture samples to be taken. Increasing the number of samples provides better quality at the cost of performance. "uModelLastFrame" - The model to world space transformation matrix of the actor in the previous frame.
members.returns: The newly created Property::Map with the motion blur effect

members.name: SetMotionStretchProperties
members.brief: Set the properties for the motion stretch.

members.name: CreateMotionStretchEffect
members.brief: Creates a new MotionStretchEffect.
members.detailed: Motion stretch shader works on a per object basis. Objects will stretch in the direction of motion when they move, or if the camera moves. Animatable/Constrainable uniforms: "uGeometryStretchFactor" - This scales the amount the geometry stretches along the motion velocity vector. A smaller value means the geometry stretches less, larger it stretches more. Default 0.5. "uSpeedScalingFactor" - This value is used to control how much to fade the actor near the edges, based on the speed the actor is moving. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.5. "uObjectFadeStart" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will start to fade towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the stretched actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default Vector2(0.25, 0.25), which is half way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uObjectFadeEnd" - The displacement from the centre of the actor that the actor will finish fading towards its edges. This is used to prevent an unsightly hard edge between the stretched actor and the scene. Depends on the values of the vertices in the vertex stream. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.5, which is all the way towards the edge for an ImageVisual::QUAD. "uAlphaScale" - Global scaler applied to the alpha of the actor. Used to make the stretched actor a bit more subtle and reveal a bit of the background behind it as it moves. When the actor is at rest this is not applied. Default 0.75. "uModelLastFrame" - The model to world space transformation matrix of the actor in the previous frame.
members.returns: The newly created Property::Map with the motion stretch effect

members.name: LoadAnimatedImage
members.brief: Starts an animated image loading task. @REMARK_INTERNET @REMARK_STORAGE.
members.signature: uint32_t LoadAnimatedImage(AsyncImageLoader asyncImageLoader, Dali::AnimatedImageLoading animatedImageLoading, uint32_t frameIndex, DevelAsyncImageLoader::PreMultiplyOnLoad preMultiplyOnLoad)
members.returns: The loading task id

params.name: animatedImageLoading
params.description: The AnimatedImageLoading to load animated image

params.name: frameIndex
params.description: The frame index of a frame to be loaded frame

params.name: preMultiplyOnLoad
params.description: ON if the image color should be multiplied by it's alpha. Set to OFF if there is no alpha or if the image need to be applied alpha mask.

members.name: Load
members.brief: Starts an image loading task. @REMARK_INTERNET @REMARK_STORAGE.
members.signature: uint32_t Load(AsyncImageLoader asyncImageLoader, const std::string &url, ImageDimensions dimensions, Dali::FittingMode::Type fittingMode, SamplingMode::Type samplingMode, bool orientationCorrection, DevelAsyncImageLoader::PreMultiplyOnLoad preMultiplyOnLoad)
members.returns: The loading task id

params.name: url
params.description: The URL of the image file to load

params.name: dimensions
params.description: The width and height to fit the loaded image to

params.name: fittingMode
params.description: The method used to fit the shape of the image before loading to the shape defined by the size parameter

params.name: samplingMode
params.description: The filtering method used when sampling pixels from the input image while fitting it to desired size

params.name: orientationCorrection
params.description: Reorient the image to respect any orientation metadata in its header

params.name: preMultiplyOnLoad
params.description: ON if the image color should be multiplied by it's alpha. Set to OFF if there is no alpha or if the image need to be applied alpha mask.

members.name: ApplyMask
members.brief: Starts an mask applying task. @REMARK_INTERNET @REMARK_STORAGE.
members.signature: uint32_t ApplyMask(AsyncImageLoader asyncImageLoader, Devel::PixelBuffer pixelBuffer, Devel::PixelBuffer maskPixelBuffer, float contentScale, bool cropToMask, DevelAsyncImageLoader::PreMultiplyOnLoad preMultiplyOnLoad)
members.returns: The masking task id

params.name: pixelBuffer
params.description: Pointer to raw pixel data to be masked

params.name: contentScale
params.description: The factor to scale the content

params.name: cropToMask
params.description: Whether to crop the content to the mask size

params.name: preMultiplyOnLoad
params.description: ON if the image color should be multiplied by it's alpha. Set to OFF if there is no alpha.

members.name: PixelBufferLoadedSignal
members.detailed: Connect to this signal if you want to load a PixelBuffer instead of a PixelData.
notes: Connecting to this signal prevents the emission of the ImageLoadedSignal.

members.name: GenerateUrl
members.brief: Generate a Url from frame buffer. This Url can be used in visuals to render the frame buffer.
notes: Any color textures already attached in this freme buffer are not converted to the Url by this method. This method does not check for duplicates, If same frame buffer is entered multiple times, a different URL is returned each time.
members.signature: Dali::Ui::ImageUrl GenerateUrl(const Dali::FrameBuffer frameBuffer, Pixel::Format pixelFormat, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)
members.returns: the ImageUrl representing this frame buffer

params.name: frameBuffer
params.description: the frame buffer to converted to Url

params.name: pixelFormat
params.description: the pixel format for this frame buffer

params.name: width
params.description: the width for this frame buffer

params.name: height
params.description: the height for this frame buffer

members.name: GenerateUrl
members.brief: Generate a Url from frame buffer. This Url can be used in visuals to render the frame buffer.
notes: Only an color texture already attached in this frame buffer can be convert to Url by this method. This method does not check for duplicates, If same frame buffer is entered multiple times, a different URL is returned each time.
members.signature: Dali::Ui::ImageUrl GenerateUrl(const Dali::FrameBuffer frameBuffer, uint8_t index)

params.name: frameBuffer

members.name: GenerateUrl

members.returns: the ImageUrl representing this pixel data

params.name: pixelData

params.name: preMultiplied
params.description: Whether this pixel data preMultiplied or not. Default as false.

members.name: GenerateUrl

params.name: nativeImageInterface

params.name: preMultiplied

members.name: GenerateUrl

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::Integration::ReservedTraitId
compounds.brief: Framework-reserved TraitId constants.
compounds.detailed: These are allocated once at static initialisation time (in reserved-trait-id.cpp) in a fixed order. All framework code references these by name, never by raw numeric value, so the actual numbers are irrelevant.

members.name: AddActorChild
members.brief: Adds a raw (non-View) Actor as a child of the given View.
members.detailed: View::OnChildAdd normally asserts when the added child cannot be DownCast to Ui::View. This Integration helper bypasses that check so internal modules can attach supporting Actors (clipping stencils, render-effect roots, decoration layers, etc.) without violating the View-only invariant for application code. The added Actor is NOT tracked in the View's child container and therefore is excluded from the View's measure/arrange layout pass. The caller is fully responsible for the Actor's size, position, and lifecycle.

params.name: view
params.description: The parent View to add to.

params.name: actor
params.description: The raw Actor to attach as a child.

members.name: Load
members.brief: The methods in the SyncImageLoader namespace are used to load pixel data from a URL synchronously.
members.detailed: Example: PixelDatapixelData=Ui::SyncImageLoader::Load("image_url.jpg"); //Checktheimagewasloadedwithouterror. if(pixelData) { //Dowork... } Loads an image synchronously. Note: When using this method, the following defaults will be used: fittingMode = Dali::FittingMode::DEFAULT samplingMode = SamplingMode::BOX_THEN_LINEAR orientationCorrection = true @REMARK_INTERNET @REMARK_STORAGE
members.returns: A PixelData object containing the image, or an invalid object on failure

params.description: The URL of the image file to load

members.returns: A PixelData object containing the image, or an invalid object on failure

enumvalues.name: START
enumvalues.brief: Align to the start (left/top)

enumvalues.name: CENTER
enumvalues.brief: Align to the center.

enumvalues.name: END
enumvalues.brief: Align to the end (right/bottom)

members.name: LineWrapMode
members.brief: Enumeration for line wrapping strategies.
members.detailed: Specifies how text is wrapped when the available layout width is insufficient to display the entire text on a single line.

enumvalues.name: WORD
enumvalues.brief: Wraps at word boundaries.

enumvalues.name: CHARACTER
enumvalues.brief: Wraps at individual characters.

enumvalues.name: HYPHENATION
enumvalues.brief: Wraps using hyphenation when possible.

enumvalues.name: MIXED
enumvalues.brief: Tries WORD wrapping first, then HYPHENATION, and falls back to CHARACTER.

members.name: LayoutDirectionMode
members.brief: Enumeration for determining how the text layout direction is resolved.

enumvalues.name: CONTENTS
enumvalues.brief: Determines the layout direction from the text content itself.

enumvalues.name: INHERIT
enumvalues.brief: Inherits the layout direction from the parent.

enumvalues.name: LOCALE
enumvalues.brief: Determines the layout direction based on the system locale.

members.name: LineHeightMode
members.brief: Enumeration for determining how the line height is interpreted.

enumvalues.name: RELATIVE
enumvalues.brief: The line height is calculated relative to the font size. The specified value is treated as a multiplier of the font pixel size.

enumvalues.name: ABSOLUTE
enumvalues.brief: The line height is specified as an absolute pixel value. The specified value is treated as the exact line height in pixels.

members.name: MarqueeTriggerPolicy
members.brief: Enumeration for defining when the marquee animation is triggered. Specifies the condition under which the marquee starts.

enumvalues.name: MANUAL
enumvalues.brief: Starts the marquee only when explicitly requested. The animation is triggered by calling StartMarquee().

enumvalues.name: ON_OVERFLOW
enumvalues.brief: Starts the marquee automatically when the text overflows. The animation is triggered during layout if the content exceeds the available space.

members.name: MarqueeOrientation
members.brief: Enumeration for selecting the orientation of the marquee animation. Defines whether the marquee scrolls horizontally or vertically.

enumvalues.name: HORIZONTAL
enumvalues.brief: Scrolls horizontally. The effective scroll direction is determined by the layout direction and the text direction (e.g., left-to-right or right-to-left).

enumvalues.name: VERTICAL
enumvalues.brief: Scrolls vertically. The content scrolls from bottom to top.

members.name: MarqueeStopMode
members.brief: Enumeration for defining how the marquee stops. Specifies the behavior when a stop request is issued.

enumvalues.name: IMMEDIATE
enumvalues.brief: Stops the marquee immediately. The current scrolling position is not preserved.

enumvalues.name: FINISH_LOOP
enumvalues.brief: Stops the marquee after the current loop finishes. The marquee continues until the current cycle completes, then stops at the loop boundary.

members.name: OverflowMode
members.brief: Enumeration for text overflow handling. Specifies how text is rendered when it exceeds the available layout bounds.

enumvalues.name: CLIP
enumvalues.brief: Clips the overflowing text. Text outside the layout bounds is not rendered.

enumvalues.name: ELLIPSIS
enumvalues.brief: Truncates the text with an ellipsis. Displays "..." at the end of the visible text.

members.name: FontWeight
members.brief: Alias for font weight enumeration.
members.detailed: Provides access to font weight values such as REGULAR, MEDIUM, BOLD, etc.

members.name: FontWidth
members.brief: Alias for font width enumeration.
members.detailed: Provides access to font width values such as CONDENSED, NORMAL, EXPANDED, etc.

members.name: FontSlant
members.brief: Alias for font slant enumeration.
members.detailed: Provides access to font slant values such as NORMAL, ITALIC, etc.

members.name: LINE_HEIGHT_AUTO
members.brief: Special value for automatic line height.
members.detailed: When used with SetLineHeight(), it uses the natural line height derived from font metrics.

compounds.name: Dali::Ui::TextureManager
compounds.detailed: API to interface with the texture manager Allows developers to add Textures through TextureSets to the texture manager so that visuals can use them to render

members.name: AddTexture
members.brief: Add a Texture to texture manager UI keeps the Texture handle until RemoveTexture is called.
notes: this method does not check for duplicates, if same Texture is added multiple times, a different URL is returned each time
members.returns: the Url string representing this texture

params.name: preMultiplied
params.description: Whether this external texture preMultiplied or not. Default as false.

members.name: AddTexture
members.brief: Add a TextureSet to texture manager UI keeps the TextureSet handle until RemoveTexture is called.
notes: this method does not check for duplicates, if same TextureSet is added multiple times, a different URL is returned each time
members.returns: the Url string representing this texture

params.name: preMultiplied
params.description: Whether this external texture preMultiplied or not. Default as false.

members.name: RemoveTexture
members.brief: Removes a TextureSet from the texture manager.
notes: TextureSet may still be used by visuals and kept alive by them
members.returns: the handle to the TextureSet or empty handle in case TextureSet is not found

members.name: DALI_PROPERTY_REGISTRATION_EXTERNAL_INTERNAL
members.brief: Helper macros for property registration using an external property index type.
members.detailed: These helpers allow implementation classes to register properties by reusing an externally defined property index enum, such as a public property index, instead of defining a local Property enum in the implementation class. The helper macros are intended to be used inside a type registration block where a local variable named typeRegistration is available, for example: DALI_TYPE_REGISTRATION_BEGIN(LabelImpl,ViewImpl,Create) DALI_PROPERTY_REGISTRATION_EXTERNAL(Ui::Text,LabelPropertyIndex,Ui::Integration,LabelImpl,"text",STRING,TEXT) DALI_TYPE_REGISTRATION_END() Registers a property using an external property index definition.
